[90027-4] Total score [PsoSat] -- **Component:** Total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** PsoSat  **Definition:** The PsoSat contains eight questions rated on a 5-point Likert and is used to determine how satisfied the patient is with their treatment or whether it needs to be optimized from their point of view. [90028-2] Routine Assessment of Patient Index Data 3 [RAPID3] -- **Component:** Routine Assessment of Patient Index Data 3 **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** RAPID3  **Definition:** The Routine Assessment of Patient Index Data 3 (RAPID-3)® includes a subset of core variables found in the Multi-dimensional HAQ (MD-HAQ), including an assessment of physical function, a patient global assessment (PGA) for pain, and a PGA for global health. The final weighted RAPID3® score is used to determine the patient's rheumatoid arthritis severity status of near remission, low severity, moderate severity, or high severity.[PMID: 21485024]

[90038-1] Weighted score [RAPID3] -- **Component:** Weighted score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** RAPID3  **Definition:** The RAPID3® is an index to assess and monitor patients with rheumatoid arthritis, without formal joint counts.[PMID: 19962621] The weighted RAPID3® score corresponds to disease activity level[LOINC: 90094-4]. [90040-7] Alpha globin gene cluster and HS-40 region deletion and duplication mutation analysis in Blood or Tissue by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** Alpha globin gene cluster & HS-40 region deletion+duplication **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** Alpha globin genes + HS-40 Del+Dup Bld/T  **Definition:** This assay detects the dosage (deletion and duplication) of alpha globin genes, including HBZ, HBM, HBA2, HBA1, HBQ1, located on the short (p) arm of chromosome 16 at position 13.3. The assay can also be used to detect the presence of deletions in the upstream LCR/HS-40 regulatory region. Deletions of the HBA1 and/or HBA2 genes are the most common cause of alpha thalassemia and in rare cases, mutations in or near these genes can also cause the disease. [GHR condition: alpha-thalassemia]

[90094-4] Rheumatoid arthritis disease severity level [RAPID3] -- **Component:** Rheumatoid arthritis disease severity level **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** RAPID3  **Definition:** The RAPID3® is an index to assess and monitor patients with rheumatoid arthritis, without formal joint counts.[PMID: 19962621] The cumulative score is the sum of the results of the 10 physical function questions reduced to a score between 0 and 10, the score for pain severity [LOINC: 90036-5], and the patient's global assessment of overall health [LOINC: 90037-3]. The cumulative score is converted to a weighted score that is interpreted as follows: 0-1.0 is near remission (NR); 1.3-2.0 as low severity (LS); 2.3-4.0 as moderate severity (MS); and 4.3-10.0 as high severity (HS). Alternatively, the activity level may be based on the RAPID3 cumulative score as follows: 1-3 is near remission; 4-6 as low severity; 7-12 as moderate severity; and 13-30 as high severity.[https://www.rheumatology.org/Portals/0/Files/RAPID3%20Form.pdf]

[90101-7] Internal control result -- **Component:** Internal control result **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Specimen **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Int control result  **Definition:** This term is used to report the result for a lab test's internal control, which is also known as a built-in or procedural control, along with the primary patient result. Internal controls are used to evaluate various aspects of the test procedure, such as whether enough specimen was added or whether the specimen migrated through the test strip. They are built in to each individual testing device (e.g., card or test strip) and provide a pass/fail result when that device is used. Internal controls are different from external controls in that external controls are not built in to the test itself but rather are included with the test kit or sold separately, usually come as both positive and negative, and are used in place of a patient specimen to run the test in order to monitor whether it is working properly. [https://www.cdc.gov/mmwr/preview/mmwrhtml/rr5413a1.htm]

[90221-3] Total score [CUDOS] -- **Component:** Total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** CUDOS  **Definition:** The Clinically Useful Depression Outcome Scale (CUDOS) score correlates with the severity of depression with higher scores indicating more severe depression. A CUDOS total score ranging between 0-10 is considered nondepressed, 11-20 minimal depression, 21-30 is mild depression, 31-45 moderate depression, and 46 and above indicates severe depression.[PMID: 18243884]
[90222-1] Clinically Useful Depression Outcome Scale [CUDOS] -- **Component:** Clinically Useful Depression Outcome Scale **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** CUDOS  **Definition:** Clinically Useful Depression Outcome Scale (CUDOS) contains 18 items assessing all of the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders, Fourth Edition (DSM-IV) inclusion criteria for major depressive disorder (MDD) and dysthymic disorder as well as psychosocial impairment and quality of life. The CUDOS score correlates with the severity of depression with higher scores indicating more severe depression.[PMID: 18243884]
[90223-9] Neutrophils.immature/Neutrophils.total [Pure number fraction] in Blood -- **Component:** Neutrophils.immature/Neutrophils.total **Property:** NFr.DF **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Neut.immature/Neut.total NFr.DF Bld  **Definition:** When evaluating newborns for sepsis, it has been found that the ratio of immature neutrophils to total neutrophils (I/T) in the peripheral blood can be a useful indicator. Several parameters are used to predict infection including a low white blood cell count (WBC), a low absolute neutrophil count (ANC), and a high immature neutrophil to total neutrophil count (I/T). Because of the expected variability of white blood cell counts in the first four hours after birth, it is recommended that the neutrophil count be done after the baby is four hours old. Low ANC indicate the highest risk for sepsis regardless of the I/T. High I/T levels only indicate a high likelihood of infection if the ANC is low. However, the effect of the I/T at various levels of ANC is less consistent. When the I/T is less than 0.15, the likelihood of infection is low regardless of the ANC.[PMID: 24503598]

The M band is present during acute infection, but can also be present during chronic infection and be detectable for months or years after the infection has resolved. [90263-5] Microscopic observation [Identifier] in Milk by Gram stain -- **Component:** Observation **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Milk **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Gram stain  **Short name:** Gram Stn Mlk  **Definition:** Milk specimens can be tested for infectious organisms as part of regulatory testing, quality control, or during an infectious disease outbreak. [90423-5] Microorganism preliminary growth detection panel - Blood by Culture -- **Component:** Microorganism preliminary growth detection panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** - **Method:** Culture  **Short name:** Microorg prel grow det pnl Bld Cult  **Definition:** This panel includes the individual terms for reporting whether or not a viable microorganism is present in a patient specimen based on different culture methods. A single specimen may be split among one or more types of culture devices (e.g., aerobic and anaerobic bottles), depending on the clinical context. The results for the terms in this panel are typically exchanged between laboratory devices and/or stored within the laboratory information system (LIS/LIMS). This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the bioMerieux BacT culture bottles for the recovery and detection of microorganisms (bacteria and fungi) from blood specimens.

[90426-8] Microorganism preliminary growth [Presence] in Blood by Aerobic culture with antimicrobial neutralizer -- **Component:** Microorganism preliminary growth **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Aerobic culture+Antimicrobial neutralizer  **Short name:** Microorg prel Bld Ql Aer cult + neut  **Definition:** This term represents the preliminary step of the culture process that is used to determine whether or not a viable aerobic microorganism is present in the specimen. At the present time (2018), this result would typically be transmitted between laboratory devices and/or stored in the laboratory information system (LIS/LIMS), but it would not be used to order a culture or report culture results to the patient's electronic health record (EHR). Rather, the corresponding LOINC code with Property Prid and Scale Nom is used to order the culture, report this preliminary result, and report the results of any subsequent organism identification tests that are performed. This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the bioMerieux BacT culture bottles for the recovery and detection of aerobic microorganisms (bacteria and fungi).

[90432-6] Microorganism preliminary growth [Presence] in Platelets from Blood product unit by Anaerobe culture -- **Component:** Microorganism preliminary growth **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Platelets^BPU **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Anaerobic culture  **Short name:** Microorg prel Plts BPU Ql Anaer Cult  **Definition:** This term represents the preliminary step of the culture process that is used to determine whether or not a viable anaerobic microorganism is present in the specimen. At the present time (2018), this result would typically be transmitted between laboratory devices and/or stored in the laboratory information system (LIS/LIMS), but it would not be used to order a culture or report culture results to the patient's electronic health record (EHR). Rather, the corresponding LOINC code with Property Prid and Scale Nom is used to order the culture, report this preliminary result, and report the results of any subsequent organism identification tests that are performed. [90730-3] Self-perception of body weight -- **Component:** Self-perception of body weight **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Self-perception of body wgt  **Definition:** This term represents a patient's self-perception of their body weight in terms of being overweight or underweight. The term is based on, but not limited in use to, one of the questions in a two-item measure for an individual's weight change goals used in the Youth Risk Behavior Survey (YRBS), is a national school-based survey conducted by CDC and state, territorial, tribal, and local education and health agencies as part of the Youth Risk Behavior Surveillance System (YRBSS). The YRBSS monitors health-related behaviors that contribute to the leading causes of death and disability among youth and adults.[https://www.cdc.gov/healthyyouth/data/yrbs/] For the other question item on what the individual is trying to do about their weight, use Body weight goal [LOINC: 90734-5].

[90734-5] Body weight change goal -- **Component:** Body weight change goal **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Body weight change goal  **Definition:** This term represents a patient's goal (e.g. gain, lose, stay the same) for their body weight. The term is based on, but not limited in use to, one of the questions in a two-item measure for an individual's weight change goals used in the Youth Risk Behavior Survey (YRBS), is a national school-based survey conducted by CDC and state, territorial, tribal, and local education and health agencies as part of the Youth Risk Behavior Surveillance System (YRBSS). The YRBSS monitors health-related behaviors that contribute to the leading causes of death and disability among youth and adults.[https://www.cdc.gov/healthyyouth/data/yrbs/] For the other question item on how the individual describes their weight, use Self-perception of body weight [LOINC: 90730-3].
[90735-2] PNH GPI-Linked WBC and RBC Ag Panel - Blood -- **Component:** Paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria GPI-Linked WBC & RBC Ag Panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** -  **Short name:** PNH GPI-Lkd WBC + RBC Ag Pnl Bld  **Definition:** The tests in this panel detect glycosylphosphatidylinositol (GPI)-linked proteins including CD14 for monocytes, CD16 and 24 for granulocytes, and CD59 for erythrocytes. In addition, fluorescent aerolysin (FLAER) which selectively binds to GPI anchor proteins, is also used to detect the presence or absence of those anchors. Paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria (PNH) is an acquired hematologic disorder of erythroid, granulocytic, and megakaryocytic cell lines. The abnormal cells in PNH are missing the GPI-linked proteins due to mutations in the phosphatidylinositol glycan A gene (PIGA).

[90738-6] Erythrocytes.CD59 complete loss/Erythrocytes in Blood -- **Component:** Erythrocytes.CD59 complete loss/Cells.235a **Property:** NFr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** RBC CD59 complete loss NFr  **Definition:** This assay measures the percent of red blood cells that have complete loss of the CD59 antigen. The loss of CD59 is associated with paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria (PNH), and patients with complete loss of CD59 are more likely to have hemolysis compared to patients with partial loss of CD59.

[90802-0] Medication volume of distribution Calculated -- **Component:** Medication volume of distribution **Property:** Vol **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated  **Short name:** Med Vd Calc  **Definition:** The volume of distribution (Vd) of a given drug is the theoretical estimate of the volume of plasma that a specific dose of drug would be contained in if all of the drug remained in the circulation; it is calculated based on the drug dose and drug concentration at time 0. The Vd is affected by properties of the drug, such as whether it is hydrophilic or lypophilic, as well as patient characteristics, including weight and height, volume status, and liver and kidney function.

[90873-1] Malocclusion severity assessment -- **Component:** Malocclusion severity assessment **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Teeth and gum **Scale:** Doc  **Short name:** Malocclusion severity assess Doc  **Definition:** A malocclusion severity assessment, such as the Salzmann Evaluation Index, is composed of a number of observations and measurements and used to help allocate treatment resources when resources are limited. This assessment record (not an examination) is intended to state whether a handicapping malocclusion is present and assess its severity according to the criteria assigned to them. Point values may be assigned based on the effect of the malocclusion on dental health, function, and aesthetics. The assessment is not directed to ascertain the presence of occlusal deviations ordinarily included in epidemiological surveys of malocclusion. Etiology, diagnosis, planning, complexity of treatment, and prognosis are not factors in this assessment.

[90918-4] Fatty acid omega-3 and omega-6 panel - Blood -- **Component:** Fatty acid omega-3 & omega-6 panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** -  **Short name:** FA omega-3 + omega-6 panel Bld  **Definition:** This panel is used to report the results for omega-3 and omega-6 testing in patients with known cardiovascular disease (CVD) or those with risk factors for CVD. It includes individual measurements for omega-3 and omega-6 fatty acids as well as total omega-3 and omega-6 levels and clinically relevant ratios. This panel was created for, but is not limited in use to, the submitter's OmegaCheck panel performed at the Cleveland Heart Lab. Note that this panel is for whole blood testing; a similar LOINC panel for Ser/Plas testing is [LOINC: 88884-2]
[90919-2] Coagulation factor VIII.recombinant.bound/von Willebrand factor (vWf) Ag in Plasma by Immunoassay -- **Component:** Coagulation factor VIII.recombinant.bound/von Willebrand factor Ag **Property:** RelRto **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Plas **Scale:** Qn **Method:** IA  **Short name:** Fact VIII.recomb.bound/vWF Ag Plas IA  **Definition:** This test measures the binding activity of von Willebrand factor (vWF) to Factor VIII as the ratio of the amount of exogenous recombinant Factor VIII bound to vWF in the patient sample to the amount of vWF in the patient sample. It is used to differentiate between various bleeding disorders including hemophilia A and type 2N von Willebrand disease (2N vWD, VWD2N, or Type 2 Normandy). Type 2N vWD is caused by mutations to the Factor VIII binding site of vWF, which results in reduced binding and faster clearance of Factor VIII. Patients with type 2N vWD usually have reduced Factor VIII activity with normal VWF levels. [PMID: 19298374]

[90928-3] Physical findings of Mandible condyle -- **Component:** Physical findings.mandibular condyle **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Mandible **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Physical findings of Mandible condyle  **Definition:** Abnormal temporomandibular joint structures, especially the mandibular condyles, affect oral function, the plane of dental occlusion and overall facial bone relationships. Aplasia, bifidity and trifidity are different anomalies affecting mandibular condyle. Bifidity and trifidity are characterized by duplicity of the mandibular condyle and are uncommon conditions.[PMID: 12582368]

[90930-9] Overbite teeth [#] -- **Component:** Overbite teeth **Property:** Num **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Teeth and gum **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Overbite teeth  **Definition:** The number of incisor teeth with an overbite (vertical overlap) in the maxilla. For an overbite, the lower incisal edges typically contact the lingual surface of the upper incisors at or above the cingulum.[NLM Catalog ID: 101578397]
[90931-7] Openbite teeth [#] -- **Component:** Openbite teeth **Property:** Num **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Teeth and gum **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Openbite teeth  **Definition:** The number of incisor teeth with an openbite in the maxilla. An openbite is the separation of upper teeth to lower teeth (no contact with) and is measured at each tooth typically in millimeters.

The examples provided in the answer list associated with this term are just a few since there are many conditions that can be reported as a severe traumatic deviation.

[91062-0] Bacteria identified in Platelets from Blood product unit by Anaerobe culture -- **Component:** Bacteria **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Platelets^BPU **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Anaerobic culture  **Short name:** Bacteria Plts BPU Anaerobe Cult  **Definition:** One of the most common causes of transfusion-related deaths is bacterial infection due to contaminated blood products. Platelets are affected more than other blood products because the temperature at which platelets are stored (20 to 24 degrees Celsius) allows bacterial growth, whereas storage temperatures of other products, such as packed red blood cells (RBC) that are stored at 1 to 6 degrees Celsius, do not. [PMID: 28066700] According to the CDC, approximately 1 in 1,000-3,000 units of platelets is affected. To address this, the AABB standard 5.1.1.1 "Standards for blood Banks and Transfusion Services, 22nd edition" requires accredited institutions to implement procedures for detecting and limiting platelet bacterial contamination. [https://www.cdc.gov/bloodsafety/bbp/bacterial-contamination-of-platelets.html]
[91063-8] Bacteria identified in Platelets from Blood product unit by Aerobe culture -- **Component:** Bacteria **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Platelets^BPU **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Aerobic culture  **Short name:** Bacteria Plts BPU Aerobe Cult  **Definition:** One of the most common causes of transfusion-related deaths is bacterial infection due to contaminated blood products. Platelets are affected more than other blood products because the temperature at which platelets are stored (20 to 24 degrees Celsius) allows bacterial growth, whereas storage temperatures of other products, such as packed red blood cells (RBC) that are stored at 1 to 6 degrees Celsius, do not. [PMID: 28066700] According to the CDC, approximately 1 in 1,000-3,000 units of platelets is affected. To address this, the AABB standard 5.1.1.1 "Standards for blood Banks and Transfusion Services, 22nd edition" requires accredited institutions to implement procedures for detecting and limiting platelet bacterial contamination. [https://www.cdc.gov/bloodsafety/bbp/bacterial-contamination-of-platelets.html]

[91120-6] Antithrombin in Platelet poor plasma by Chromo.no addition of heparin -- **Component:** Antithrombin **Property:** RelCCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** PPP **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Chromo.no addition of heparin  **Short name:** AT III PPP Chromo.no added heparin  **Definition:** This term represents an antithrombin (AT) assay which, in contrast to typical AT assays, is performed in the ABSENCE of heparin. This assay is also known as "Progressive antithrombin assay." It is used to identify type IIb AT deficiency, which is caused by a mutation in the AT heparin binding site (HBS). In type IIb AT deficiency, AT activity is reduced in the presence of heparin but is normal in the absence of heparin.[PMID: 19141163] This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the Biophen Antithrombin 5 assay's VARIANT Method, which is performed without heparin. In patients with type IIb AT deficiency, AT activity is expected to be normal in patients with an HBS mutation.

[91124-8] Platelet function with adenosine diphosphate induction panel - Blood by Resonance TEG -- **Component:** Platelet function with adenosine diphosphate induction panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** - **System:** Bld **Scale:** - **Method:** Thromboelastography.resonance  **Short name:** Platelet func ADP-ind Pnl Bld Res TEG  **Definition:** This panel is used to report results for adenosine diphosphate (ADP)-induced platelet function testing by resonance thromboelastography (TEG). It includes terms for reporting maximum clot strength potential, individual clot strength based on fibrin and platelet activation by ADP, relative strength due to ADP's activating effects, and the relative strength that would remain in the presence of anti-platelet ADP-receptor inhibiting drugs. This panel was created for, but is not limited in use to, the Submitter's test kit, the TEG6s PlateletMapping ADP Assay Cartridge for heparinized blood.
[91125-5] Maximum clot strength amplitude [Length] in Blood by Thromboelastography.resonance.kaolin induced.after addition of heparinase -- **Component:** Maximum clot strength amplitude **Property:** Len **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Thromboelastography.resonance.kaolin induced.after addition of heparinase  **Short name:** Maximum clot strength amplitude Bld Thromboelastography.resonance.kaolin induced.after addition of heparinase  **Definition:** The maximum clot strength in the presence of kaolin and heparinase, as measured by the amplitude of resonance of the blood meniscus as the blood sample clots. In this test, heparinase neutralizes any heparin present in the sample so that thrombin activity is not blocked; the combination of kaolin and thrombin stimulates maximum platelet activity and clot generation, which is used as a baseline measure of maximum clot strength potential. This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the Submitter's test kit, the TEG6s PlateletMapping ADP Assay Cartridge for heparinized blood.
[91126-3] Maximum clot strength amplitude [Length] in Blood by Thromboelastography.resonance.activator F induced -- **Component:** Maximum clot strength amplitude **Property:** Len **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Thromboelastography.resonance.activator F induced  **Short name:** Maximum clot strength amplitude Bld Thromboelastography.resonance.activator F induced  **Definition:** The maximum clot strength in the presence of activator F, as measured by the amplitude of resonance of the blood meniscus as the blood sample clots. In this test, thrombin activity is blocked, and Activator F is used to activate fibrinogen in order to measure the clot strength based on fibrin. This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the Submitter's test kit, the TEG6s PlateletMapping ADP Assay Cartridge for heparinized blood.
[91127-1] Maximum clot strength amplitude [Length] in Blood by Thromboelastography.resonance.adenosine diphosphate induced -- **Component:** Maximum clot strength amplitude **Property:** Len **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Thromboelastography.resonance.adenosine diphosphate induced  **Short name:** Maximum clot strength amplitude Bld Thromboelastography.resonance.adenosine diphosphate induced  **Definition:** The maximum clot strength in the presence of adenosine diphosphate, as measured by the amplitude of resonance of the blood meniscus as the blood sample clots. In this test, thrombin activity is blocked, and adenosine diphosphate (ADP) is used to activate platelets in order to measure the clot strength based on platelet activation by ADP, which result is used to determine the relative contribution of ADP-induced platelet activation [LOINC: 91128-9] to the overall clot strength [LOINC: 91125-5]. This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the Submitter's test kit, the TEG6s PlateletMapping ADP Assay Cartridge for heparinized blood.
[91128-9] Clot strength [Length fraction] in Blood by Thromboelastography.platelet adenosine diphosphate receptor inhibited -- **Component:** Clot strength **Property:** LenFr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Thromboelastography.platelet adenosine diphosphate receptor inhibited  **Short name:** Clot strength LenFr Bld TEG. ADP recep inh  **Definition:** Calculation of percentage of clot strength that is lost due to inhibition of adenosine diphosphate (ADP)-induced platelet  aggregation. This is calculated based on measurements of the patient's maximum clot strength and clot strength due to ADP-induced platelet aggregation, via methods such as thrombelastography or resonance thromboelastography. It is used to guide anti-platelet therapy decisions.
[91129-7] Clot strength [Length fraction] in Blood by Thromboelastography.adenosine diphosphate induced -- **Component:** Clot strength **Property:** LenFr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Thromboelastography.adenosine diphosphate induced  **Short name:** Clot strength ADP-ind LenFr Bld Res TEG  **Definition:** Calculation of percentage of clot strength due to the platelet activating effects of adenosine disphosphate (ADP).  This is calculated based on measurements of the patient's maximum clot strength and clot strength due to ADP-induced platelet aggregation, via methods such as thrombelastography or resonance thromboelastography. Used to guide anti-platelet therapy decisions.

[91135-4] Acanthocytes [Presence] in Urine sediment by Light microscopy -- **Component:** Acanthocytes **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Urine sed **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Microscopy.light  **Short name:** Acanthocytes UrnS Ql Micro  **Definition:** Acanthocytes are a type of dysmorphic red blood cell (RBC) with vesicle-shaped protrusions. The presence of acanthocytes in urine sediment is indicative of glomerular pathology. Although hematuria is common in diabetic nephropathy, acanthocytosis is not; therefore, patients with diabetic nephropathy who are found to have acanthocytes in their urine sediment should have further work-up to determine the etiology of glomerular disease. [PMID: 14693988]

[91150-3] Pressure injury stage NPUAP -- **Component:** Pressure injury stage **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom **Method:** NPUAP  **Short name:** Pressure injury stage NPUAP  **Definition:** In 2016, the National Pressure Ulcer Advisory Panel (NPUAP) redefined the definition of a pressure ulcer, transitioning to the use "injury" instead of "ulcer". As defined by the NPUAP, a pressure injury is localized damage to the skin and underlying soft tissue usually over a bony prominence or related to a medical or other device. The injury can present as intact skin or an open ulcer and may be painful. The injury occurs as a result of intense and/or prolonged pressure or pressure in combination with shear.

[91349-1] STarT Back Screening Tool panel -- **Component:** STarT Back Screening Tool panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** STarT Back  **Definition:** The Keele STarT Back Screening Tool (SBST) is a 9-item questionnaire that helps clinicians identify modifiable risk factors (biomedical, psychological, and social) for back pain disability. The resulting score stratifies patients into low, medium or high risk categories for persistent symptoms.
[91350-9] Distress subscore [STarT Back] -- **Component:** Distress subscore **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** STarT Back  **Definition:** The Keele STarT Back Screening Tool distress subscore is the sum of items 5 through 9. If a patient's total score [LOINC: 91351-7] is greater than or equal to 4, then the distress subscore differentiates between medium risk (subscore of 3 or less) and high risk (subscore of 4 or more) of developing long-term back pain. The distress subscore is also referred to as the "psych score" in Keele documentation.
[91351-7] Total score [STarT Back] -- **Component:** Total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** STarT Back
[91352-5] STarT Back Screening risk level -- **Component:** STarT Back Screening risk level **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord **Method:** STarT Back  **Definition:** The Keele STarT Back Screening Tool (SBST) risk level categorizes patients into low, medium, or high risk of developing long-term back problems. If a patient's total score [LOINC:91351-7] is 3 or less, the patient is in the low risk category. If a patient's total score [LOINC:91351-7] is 4 or more, then the distress subscore [LOINC:91350-9] differentiates between medium risk (subscore of 3 or less) and high risk (subscore of 4 or more) categories.

[91392-1] Total score ORT -- **Component:** Total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** ORT  **Short name:** Total score ORT  **Definition:** The Opioid Risk Tool (ORT) total score categorizes patients as low (score 0-3), medium (score 4-7), or high (score 8 or more) risk for opioid abuse. Validation of this tool has shown that patients in the low risk category are unlikely to abuse opioids, patients with moderate risk have an equal likelihood of abusing and not abusing opioids, and patients with high risk are likely to abuse opioids. [PMID: 16336480]

[91585-0] Reliability of information source -- **Component:** Reliability of information source **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Event **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Reliability of info source  **Definition:** The level of reliability of the source of information. For example, a source that has no doubt of authenticity, trustworthiness, or competency and has a history of providing completely accurate information is labeled as "reliable" whereas a source that lacks these attributes and has a history of invalid information is reported as "unreliable". Sources that are doubted for being authentic, trustworthy, or competent but have provided valid information in the past are labeled as "fairly reliable".

[91590-0] Mycobacterium preliminary growth [Presence] in Body fluid by Organism specific culture -- **Component:** Mycobacterium preliminary growth **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Body fld **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Organism specific culture  **Short name:** Mycobact prelim grow Fld Ql Cult  **Definition:** This term represents the preliminary step of the culture process that is used to determine whether or not viable Mycobacteria are present in the specimen. At the present time (2019), this result would typically be transmitted between laboratory devices and/or stored in the laboratory information system (LIS/LIMS), but it would not be used to order a culture or report culture results to the patient's electronic health record (EHR). Rather, the corresponding LOINC code with Property Prid and Scale Nom is used to order the culture, report this preliminary result, and report the results of any subsequent organism identification tests that are performed. This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the bioMerieux BacT culture bottles for the recovery and detection of Mycobacteria.

[91629-6] General Well-Being Schedule - 18 item [NHANES] -- **Component:** General Well-Being Schedule - 18 item **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** NHANES  **Definition:** The General Well Being (GWB) Schedule 18-item self-administered questionnaire was initially developed for the U.S. Health and Nutrition Examination Survey (HANES I). The GWB Schedule includes positive and negative questions across six dimensions: well-being, self-control, vitality, depression, anxiety, and general health. Each item has the time frame "during the last month," and the first 14 questions use 6-point response scales representing intensity or frequency. The remaining four questions use 0-10 rating scales defined by adjectives at each end. The polarity of items 1, 3, 6, 7, 9, 11, 15, and 16 is reversed for scoring, and items within each subscale are added together for a total score. Lower scores reflect distress and higher scores reflect positive well-being.[PMID: 610049]

[91642-9] Medical Outcomes Study Social Support Survey panel [MOS Social Support Survey] -- **Component:** Medical Outcomes Study Social Support Survey panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** MOS Social Support Survey  **Definition:** This panel contains the set of LOINCs used in the self-administered Medical Outcomes Study (MOS) Social Support Survey instrument. The MOS was a two-year study of patients with chronic conditions. The instrument used in the study includes four separate social support subscales (emotional/informational, tangible, affectionate, and positive social interaction) and an overall functional social support index. Higher scores on an individual scale or for the overall support index suggest that more support is available.[PMID: 2035047][RAND: mos/social-support]

[91651-0] General Well-Being Schedule - 18 item - total score [NHANES] -- **Component:** General Well-Being Schedule - 18 item - total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** NHANES  **Definition:** The total score for the General Well Being Schedule (GWB) 18-item, self-administered questionnaire. The first 14 questions use 6-point response scales representing intensity or frequency, where each response is assigned a numeric score from 0 to 5 (in the original GWB Schedule) or 1 to 6 (in some alternate versions). The remaining four questions use 0-10 rating scales defined by adjectives at each end. The polarity of items 1, 3, 6, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15, and 16 is reversed for scoring, and if using the original GWB Schedule, scores within each subscale are added together for a total score. If the first 14 questions are scored using the alternate 1 to 6 scoring then 14 is substracted from the total. Regardless of the scoring system used, the total score ranges from 0-110. Lower scores reflect distress and higher scores reflect positive well-being.[PMID: 610049]

[91663-5] Social support index [MOS Social Support Survey] -- **Component:** Social support index **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** MOS Social Support Survey  **Definition:** Medical Outcomes Study (MOS) Social Support Survey consists of four separate social support subscales and an overall functional social support index (this term), which is calculated based on the subscales and additional item scores by averaging the individual scores for all 19 items. Note that the overall support index calculation does not use the individual subscale scores. Scores can be transformed to a 0-100 scale using the following formula: 100 X [(observed score - minimum possible score)/(maximum possible score - minimum possible score)]. A higher score for an individual scale or for the overall support index indicates more support is available. [RAND: mos/social-support/scoring]

[91684-1] Histoplasma capsulatum H and M Ab panel - Cerebral spinal fluid -- **Component:** Histoplasma capsulatum H & M Ab panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** CSF **Scale:** -  **Short name:** H capsulatum H+M Ab panel CSF  **Definition:** Immunodiffusion (ID) and complement fixation (CF) are used to detect antibodies to Histoplasma capsulatum in spinal fluid. The ID assay detects the presence of antibodies to the H. capsulatum M and H precipitin bands. The M band is present during acute infection, but can also be present during chronic infection and be detectable for months or years after the infection has resolved. The H band is present during chronic or severe acute infections. The M band is usually also present when the H band is detected. [PMID: 17223625]

[91890-4] Emergency supply category -- **Component:** Emergency supply category **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Supply **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Emergency supply category  **Definition:** Defines the category (e.g. medical materials, equipment, drugs/medications) of an emergency supply item.
[91891-2] Facility Bed type -- **Component:** Bed type **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Facility **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Facility Bed type  **Definition:** The type of beds available at a facility. For example, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) terminology categorizes bed types based on the National Hospital Available Beds for Emergencies and Disasters (HAvBED) reporting system.[https://www.hsdl.org/?view&did=484620] In this system, beds are categorized as follows: Adult Intensive Care Unit beds (beds that can support critically ill/injured patients, including ventilator support), adult medical/surgical beds (also thought of as Adult "Ward" beds), burn care beds or burn ICU beds (either approved by the American Burn Association or self-designated), pediatric ICU beds (for patients 17 years and younger), pediatric medical/surgical beds (for patients 17 and younger), psychiatric beds (beds on a closed/locked psychiatric unit or ward where a patient will be staffed by an attendant), airborne infection isolation beds (also called "negative pressure/isolation" with negative airflow and providing respiratory isolation), and operating room beds.

[92049-6] Staphylococcus species methicillin resistant identified in Isolate or Specimen by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** Staphylococcus sp.methicillin resistant **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Isolate/Specimen **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** Staph methicillin resist Islt/Spm  **Definition:** The detection of methicillin resistant Staphylococcus species (MRSA), MSSA (methicillin-sensitive Staphylococcus aureus) and/or CoNS (Coagulase-negative Staphylococcus) in isolates or clinical specimens, such as nasal/throat, wound, groin and perineum swabs. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the Bosch Vivalytic MRSA assay, a multiplex real-time PCR used for the detection MRSA/SA infections in healthcare settings. [92252-6] Microorganism molecular resistance panel by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** Microorganism molecular resistance panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Isolate/Specimen **Scale:** - **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** Micro molecular resistance Pnl Islt/Spm  **Definition:** This panel is used to report the results for molecular microorganism resistance testing and includes terms to report the resistance genes, target sequences and/or mutations tested for and identified, as well as the predicted susceptibility or resistance to one or more antimicrobials based on the genotyping results. The panel includes a representative set of LOINC terms for various antimicrobials, but any LOINC term for antimicrobial susceptibility by genotyping may be used as appropriate depending on genes and/or mutations tested for. Note that a single instance of the terms included in the panel is used to report the results for a single resistance gene or mutation, so if more than one gene or mutation was tested for, the panel should be repeated for each one. For example, if a particular assay targets both the rpoB and katG resistance genes for Mycobacterium tuberculosis complex, then the first instance of the panel would be used to report the molecular results for rpoB and predicted susceptibility for rifampin, and the second instance would be used for katG and isoniazid. If using HL7 v2 messaging, the results would be linked using OBX-4.
[92253-4] Microorganism identified in Isolate or Specimen by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** Microorganism **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Isolate/Specimen **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** Microorganism Islt/Spm

This panel includes a representative set of LOINC terms for various antimicrobials, but any LOINC term for antimicrobial susceptibility by genotyping may be used as appropriate depending on genes and/or mutations tested for.

[92821-8] Allelic frequency in Population -- **Component:** Allelic frequency **Property:** NFr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Population **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Allelic freq NFr Pop  **Definition:** The allelic frequency is the relative frequency (or fraction) of the given allele reported at the locus of interest in a given population. It is usually expressed as a number from 0 to 1. (https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/neuroscience/allele-frequency)  Allelic frequency is usually reported as a decimal fraction for both Sample Variant Allelic Frequency and Population Allelic Frequency, although it is occasionally reported as a percent. Special care/caution should be taken when reporting and converting to a decimal fraction.
[92822-6] Genomic coordinate system [Type] -- **Component:** Genomic coordinate system **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** Genomic coord system  **Definition:** A genomic coordinate is a position along a sequence. Coordinate systems can start counting from:    0-based interval counting  - Used by: GA4GH API, ClinGen data model, BED & BAM files, UCSC files, HLM 1.0 and NCBI's SPDI conversion format  - Akin to cursor positioning in modern text editors with cursor between characters  - A bit unnatural for humans, easier for computers   - Sequences have an inclusive start and exclusive end  - Insertions always go in the interval between characters      0-based character counting  - Used by: Genbank database & ASN files      - Sequences have an inclusive start, exclusive end  - Length = end - start  - A bit unnatural for human reading, easier for computers  - Sequences have an inclusive start and inclusive end  - Insertions not defined because GenBank and ASN do not model insertions to records they contain (historically ASN did internal to NCBI). SPDI was developed in part because of this inability to model insertions.    1-based character counting   - Used by: HGVS, VCF, NCBI's ClinVar (uses HGVS), Genbank files, IPD-IMGT/HLA, UCSC genome browser (note different from UCSC file, which uses 0-based interval counting)  - Akin to approach used in the earliest text processors  - Length = (end - start) + 1  - Sequences have inclusive start and inclusive end  - More natural for humans  - Insertions. Be careful in theory for 1-base, the insertion location could be defined in three ways: a) before position, b) after position, c) specify the di-nucleotide which where the insertion goes; but in practice there is only one way to do it per coordinate type.    Distinctions for describing sub sequences with the three approaches with examples  Consider ACGTAGTC as an example string and what nucleotides would be in the range 2-4   - 0-based interval counting (e.g..SPDI): GT  - 0-based character counting (i.e. GenBank): GTA  - 1-based character counting (i.e. VCF): CGT    Distinctions regarding insertions with examples. Insertions can be tricky with character counting.  Now consider that you want to describe an insertion between T&A (4th and 5th characters in the example string):  - 0-based interval counting (i.e. SPDI) insert at position 4.  - 0-based character counting. Insert between 3&4. Note: insertions are not supported in GenBank's model.  - 1-based character counting. Insert between 4&5. Note: HGVS requires specification of the dinucleotide between which the insertion goes. For VCF, this would be represented as insert after 4 and does not require other information but the result is the same.

[92840-8] Emergency operations standard operating procedure Document -- **Component:** Emergency operations standard operating procedure **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Event **Scale:** Doc  **Short name:** Emergency operations SOP Doc  **Definition:** Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) contain formal, written guidelines for an emergency or incident response that may include both operational and technical components. SOPs describes the concept of operations and scope of procedures for a public health emergency operations center (EOC). The scope may include responsibilities of personnel, assigned duties in the EOC and checklists of items that should be accomplished throughout the course of the EOC operations.

[92843-2] FLT3 gene p.Asp835 mutations [Presence] in Blood or Tissue by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** FLT3 gene.p.Asp835 mutations **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** FLT3 p.D835 mutations Bld/T Ql  **Definition:** This test detects, but does not distinguish between, mutations in the FLT3 gene at the D835 position. [92844-0] FLT3 gene internal tandem duplication/Normal [Ratio] in Blood or Tissue by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** FLT3 gene internal tandem duplication/normal **Property:** Ratio **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** FLT3 ITD Dp/Nor Bld/T-Rto  **Definition:** This term represents a ratio (allelic ratio), following PCR amplification, of a patient's FLT3 gene DNA having internal tandem duplication to that same patient's wild type FLT3 gene DNA not having such duplication. The result is expressed as a signal ratio of peak band intensities and correlates to cells with and without mutated DNA.  A ratio of 100.00 is the maximum and indicates a patient specimen has 100% FLT3-mutated tumor with no healthy cells.  A ratio between 0.03 and 0.05 indicates low levels of disease.
[92845-7] Insulin resistance score in Serum by Calculated.CardioIQ -- **Component:** Insulin resistance score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated.CardioIQ  **Short name:** Insulin resist score Ser Calc.CardioIQ  **Definition:** This term was created for Quest Diagnostics' CardioIQ insulin resistance score, which is part of the CardioIQ Insulin resistance panel. The insulin resistance score is calculated using a proprietary formula that is based on fasting insulin and C-peptide levels. The score can range from 0-100 and a score of less than 33 represents normal insulin sensitivity. Scores of 33-66 and greater than 66 are associated with a greater than 4-fold and greater than 15-fold increased risk, respectively, of having insulin resistance compared to patients with a score of less than 33.
[92846-5] Ambulatory blood pressure monitor study -- **Component:** Ambulatory blood pressure monitor study **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Doc  **Short name:** ABPM study  **Definition:** An ambulatory blood pressure (ABP) or ambulatory blood pressure monitoring (ABPM) study consists of multiple blood pressure readings for a patient over a period of time, typically 24 hours. ABPM is useful in evaluating patients with variable BP readings in the clinic, with discrepancies between BP readings at home and in the physician's office (i.e. "white coat" hypertension), and to obtain nocturnal BP readings. ABPM reports are used to identify cardiovascular risk and guiding therapeutic decisions. [PMID: 26049213]

[92926-5] CT Guidance for radiation treatment of Unspecified body region-- WO and W contrast IV -- **Component:** Guidance for radiation treatment^WO & W contrast IV **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** Doc **Method:** CT  **Short name:** CT Guided RT--WO+W contr IV  **Definition:** One of the first steps in radiation oncology treatment planning is radiation simulation, during which detailed imaging is done to show the precise location of a patient's tumor and the surrounding healthy tissue. This information guides treatment planning in that it is used to determine the optimal patient positioning, to mark the patient's skin with small dots of permanent ink (tattoos) to ensure that the same precise area is treated each time, and to create custom immobilization devices such as a face mask or a leg mold. Various modalities may be used for radiation simulation to guide radiation treatment planning, including computed tomography (CT) [LOINC: 79072-5] or CT WO & W contrast (this term), magnetic resonance imaging (MR) [LOINC: 82123-1] , positron emission tomography (PT) [LOINC: 82126-4] and ultrasound (US) [LOINC: 82124-9].

[93016-4] Scale for the Assessment and Rating of Ataxia [SARA] -- **Component:** Scale for the Assessment and Rating of Ataxia **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** SARA  **Short name:** SARA panel  **Definition:** The Scale for the Assessment and Rating of Ataxia (SARA) is a clinical scale used to assess cerebellar ataxia. The scale includes 8 items that are related to gait, stance, sitting, speech, finger-chase test, nose-finger test, fast alternating movements and heel-shin test. A trained physician observes the participant perform these physical function tests and gives each one a value from the scale.

[93037-0] Portable medical order form -- **Component:** Portable medical order form **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** {Setting} **Scale:** Doc **Method:** {Role}  **Short name:** Portable med order form  **Definition:** A portable medical order form, commonly called a Physician Orders for Scope of Treatment (POST), POLST (Physician Orders for Life-Sustaining Treatment), or Medical Orders for Life Sustaining Treatment (MOLST), is a provider's order based on the patient's current medical condition and preferences. The form typically includes medical orders regarding CPR-code or no code status, the level of medical intervention (comfort measures, limited additional interventions, or full intervention), the preference for use of antibiotics (for comfort only or full treatment), and the use of medically administered nutrition. A protable medical order form may be changed or revoked by the patient or their authorized healthcare decision-maker (i.e. patient advocate or court-appointed guardian) at any time.

[93044-6] Level of evidence -- **Component:** Level of evidence **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Level of evidence  **Definition:** In the interpretation of sequence variants, standard terminology is used to describe the level of evidence that supports the association between a particular genetic variant and a particular disorder or disease state. Three example answer lists for levels of evidence are listed below but we recognize that a local terminology may also be used based on other factors such as population, type of data studied, etc. The example answer list associated with this term is that given by the American College of Medical Genetics, but depending on the use case, other lists may be just as appropriate.  American College of Medical Genetics [https://www.acmg.net/docs/standards_guidelines_for_the_interpretation_of_sequence_variants.pdf] Very strong evidence pathogenic Strong evidence pathogenic Moderate evidence pathogenic Supporting evidence pathogenic Supporting evidence benign Strong evidence benign Stand-alone evidence pathogenic Stand-alone evidence benign Uncertain significance  PharmGKB CPIC Clinical Annotation Levels of Evidence [https://www.pharmgkb.org/page/clinAnnLevels] Level 1A High Level 1B High Level 2a Moderate Level 2b Moderate Level 3 Low Level 4 Preliminary  AMP guidelines to be used for Somatic Variant Interpretation/Reporting (list has meaning for Therapeutic, Diagnostic, and Prognostic uses as further described in the reference). [https://jmd.amjpathol.org/article/S1525-1578(16)30223-9/pdf] Tier 1 Level A - (Strong Clinical Significance) FDA-approved therapy and/or included in professional guidelines. Tier 1 Level B - (Strong Clinical Significance) Well-powered studies with consensus from experts in the field. Tier 2 Level C - (Potential Clinical Significance) FDA-approved therapies for different tumor types or investigational therapies, multiple small published studies with some consensus. Tier 2 Level D - (Potential Clinical Significance) Preclinical trials or a few case reports without consensus. Tier 3 - (Unknown Clinical Significance) Not observed at a significant allele frequency in general or specific subpopulation databases or pan-cancer or tumor-specific variant databases, no convincing published evidence of cancer association. Tier 4 - (Benign or Likely Benign) Observed at significant allele frequency in the general or specific subpopulation databases and no existing published evidence of cancer association.

[93084-2] NuMoM2b Difficulties in Pregnancy form [NuMoM2b] -- **Component:** Nulliparous Pregnancy Outcomes Study Monitoring Mothers-to-Be Difficulties in Pregnancy form **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** NuMoM2b  **Definition:** A single-item (with 13 subparts), Likert-style self-report questionnaire used to assess if a woman is feeling bothered, upset, or worried about various items during her pregnancy. The questionnaire was developed as part of the Nulliparous Pregnancy Outcomes Study: Monitoring Mothers-to-Be (nuMoM2b) study funded primarily by the Eunice Kennedy Shriver National Institute of Child Health and Human Development (NICHD). NuMoM2b was a prospective cohort study that collected data from the first trimester of pregnancy through the end of pregnancy on approximately 10,000 nulliparous women. [PMID: 25648779]

[93112-1] Total score [Quality of Working Life Questionnaire for Cancer Survivors] -- **Component:** Total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** QWLQ-CS  **Definition:** The total score of the Quality of Working Life Questionnaire for Cancer Survivors is the sum of the 23 item scores. The 23 items are scored on a 6-point Likert scale from Disagree completely (score = 1) to Agree completely (score = 6), except items 9, 20, 21, 22, and 23 that are reverse scored. Changes in the QWLQ-CS scores can be used to inform clinical practice, and a change of 3.9 points or greater is considered clinically relevant. [PMID:30178900]

[93119-6] Right eye Dark-mediated rod adaptation time -- **Component:** Dark-mediated rod adaptation time **Property:** Time **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Eye.right **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** R eye Dark rod adaptation tm  **Definition:** This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the Rod Intercept™ (RI™) as measured by the AdaptDx®. Rod Intercept™ is the duration between exposure to a bright light (photo bleaching using a flash) and recovery of a visual sensitivity by the eye's rod photoreceptors to a threshold of 3.0 log units of attenuation of the stimulus. The rod-intercept time is used to quantify rod-mediated dark adaptation (DA), and slower DA is associated with the early phases of age-related macular degeneration (AMD). [PMID: 26522707]    Rod Intercept™ (RI™) © 2019 MacuLogix, Inc. Used with permission.

[93122-0] Contraindicated vaccine type [Identifier] -- **Component:** Contraindicated vaccine type **Property:** ID **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Contraindicated vaccine  **Definition:** The Contraindicated Vaccine Type indicates a type of vaccine that would normally be recommended but is contraindicated for a given patient. For example, live attenuated influenza vaccine is typically an allowable vaccine for individuals 2 through 49 years of age but is contraindicated for individuals with asthma and should not be given.
[93123-8] Preferred vaccine type [Identifier] -- **Component:** Preferred vaccine type **Property:** ID **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Pref vaccine  **Definition:** The Preferred Vaccine Type indicates the best possible vaccine to administer when multiple potential vaccines are available to be given to the patient. Preference may be based on patient immunization history, product availability, provider preference or other factors.

[93125-3] Modified 30 seconds Sit to stand --with use of upper extremities -- **Component:** Sit to stand^with use of upper extremities **Property:** NRat **Time aspect:** 30S **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Modified 30s Sit to Stand w extremities  **Definition:** The number of times a patient is able to stand from a sitting position in 30 seconds with use of upper extremities. The modified 30-second Sit to Stand test (m30sSTS, also call modified 30s Chair Stand Test or 30s CST) test is similar to the traditional 30 second Sit to Stand test [LOINC: 66247-8] except the chair may contain arm rests and the patient is allowed to use their arms for support.[PMID: 28464024] [PMID: 30807554]

[93127-9] Risk adjustment factor [Score] -- **Component:** Risk adjustment factor **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** RAF Scre  **Definition:** The Risk Adjustment Factor score (RAF) is calculated by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) on a quarterly basis.  An individual patient's RAF is based on a combination of Hierarchical Condition Categories (HCCs), which are associated with ICD-10 diagnoses, and patient demographics (including gender and age).  RAFs are used to calculate annual expenditure for an individual patient and help providers and insurers determine the potential financial risks of patient care. [www.aafp.org/fpm/2016/0900/p24.html]

[93148-5] XR Ulna Bone development stage -- **Component:** Bone development stage **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Upper extremity>Ulna **Scale:** Qn **Method:** XR  **Short name:** XR Ulna Bone dev stage  **Definition:** The latest Tanner and Whitehouse Method, sometimes referred to as TW2, involves the interpretation of a radiograph of the left hand and wrist by a trained radiologist. The procedure includes the assessment of regions of interest (ROI) on 20 bones. The ROI include the epiphysis, metaphysis and diaphysis of each bone. The radiologist gives each ROI a numerical score and records the result. The numerical score of each ROI is summed to produce a total score.

[93189-9] XR Wrist and Hand Total skeletal maturity score -- **Component:** Total skeletal maturity score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Upper extremity>Wrist+Hand **Scale:** Qn **Method:** XR  **Short name:** XR Wrist+Hand Total maturity score  **Definition:** The total skeletal maturity score as determined by various bone age assessment methods, such as Tanner-Whitehouse (TW2), using x-ray. [93194-9] NUDT15 gene mutations found [Identifier] in Blood or Tissue by Molecular genetics method Nominal -- **Component:** NUDT15 gene targeted mutation analysis **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** NUDT15 gene Mut Anl Bld/T
[93195-6] NUDT15 gene product metabolic activity interpretation in Blood or Tissue Qualitative by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** NUDT15 gene product metabolic activity interpretation **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** NUDT15 gene prod met act imp Bld/T-Imp  **Definition:** The NUDT15 gene product metabolic activity interpretation is determined by the reporting lab and returned with the structured test results. It indicates the lab's interpretation of the phenotype, regardless of whether the lab assay's method was genetic or enzymatic. This specific interpretation would be considered a separate observation made by the lab in addition to the primary reported results (e.g., genotype or measured activity level) and it could be included with other assay-specific observations, which would ideally support the interpretation value.  The lab may use the Clinical Pharmacogenetics Implementation Consortium (CPIC) guidelines for reporting DPYD gene product metabolic activity (phenotype).
[93196-4] Warfarin response genotype panel - Blood or Tissue by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** Warfarin response genotype panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** - **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** Warfarin genotype pnl Bld/T  **Definition:** This panel contains genotype results for warfarin dosing. As of 2019, CYP2C9, VKORC1, CYP4F2, and rs12777823 genotypes are used when available to help determine a patient's initial therapeutic warfarin dose. VKORC1 and CYP2C9 genotypes are the most important genetic determinants of warfarin dosing. Additionally, a genetic variation in the CYP4F2 gene, CYP4F2*3, as well as a variant near the CYP2C gene cluster, rs12777823, have been associated with influencing warfarin therapy.[NCBI Books: NBK84174] This panel is based on, but not limited in use to, the 2017 Update of the Clinical Pharmacogenetics Implementation Consortium (CPIC) Guideline for Pharmacogenetics-Guided Warfarin Dosing, which provides warfarin dosing recommendations for adults with and without African ancestry, and also for pediatric patients.[https://cpicpgx.org/content/guideline/publication/warfarin/2017/warfarin.pdf]
[93197-2] CYP4F2 gene c.1297G>A [Genotype] in Blood or Tissue by Molecular genetics method Nominal -- **Component:** CYP4F2 gene.c.1297G>A **Property:** Geno **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** CYP4F2 c.1297G>A Geno Bld/T  **Definition:** The presence of the CYP4F2*3 (rs2108622) allele indicates that a patient who self-identifies as being of non-African ancestry may require a slightly increased warfarin dose than predicted by CYP2C9/VKORC1 alone.[PMID: 18250228] The rs2108622 variant (c.1297G>A) results in an amino acid substitution (valine to methionine) at position 433 (p.V433M).
[93198-0] 10q23 g.94645745G>A [Genotype] in Blood or Tissue by Molecular genetics method Nominal -- **Component:** 10q23 g.94645745G>A **Property:** Geno **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** 10q23 g.94645745G>A Geno Bld/T  **Definition:** The non-coding variant g.94645745G>A (rs12777823) located in the CYP2C cluster near the CYP2C18 gene on chromosome 10 is associated with warfarin dosing among African Americans (mainly originating from West Africa). For those with rs12777823 A/G or A/A genotypes, a warfarin dose reduction of 10-25% is recommended.[PMID: 28198005]
[93199-8] DPYD gene targeted mutation analysis in Blood or Tissue by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** DPYD gene targeted mutation analysis **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** DPYD gene Mut Anl Bld/T  **Definition:** The test includes targeted analysis of variants in the DPYD gene by various molecular pathology methods, including PCR and sequencing. Testing may be performed to identifying individuals at increased risk of toxicity when considering 5-fluorouracil (5-FU) and capecitabine chemotherapy treatment. Variations detected in the DPYD gene are also associated with dihydropyrimidine dehydrogenase (DPD) deficiency.
[93200-4] Ehlers-Danlos syndrome multigene analysis in Blood or Tissue by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** Ehlers-Danlos syndrome multigene analysis **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** EDS Multigene Anl Bld/T  **Definition:** This test includes analysis of multiple genes associated with Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome by molecular genetic methods, such as next generation sequencing and deletion/duplication analysis. Targeted genes include, but are not limited to, ADAMTS2, ATP7A, CHST14, COL1A1, COL1A2, COL3A1, COL5A1, COL5A2, FKBP14, FLNA, and PLOD1. Other labs test more (or fewer) genes. The overall report typically includes the gene evaluated, variant(s) detected, interpretation, test methodology, and recommendations. Mayo website now lists 12 genes (previously 11). The count will likely increase (one lab does 18).
[93201-2] Coronary heart disease multigene analysis in Blood or Tissue by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** Coronary heart disease multigene analysis **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** CHD Multigene Anl Bld/T  **Definition:** This test includes analysis of alleles at multiple genetic loci associated with coronary heart disease (CHD). Testing is performed to determine an individual's genetic-based risk for CHD, particularly in patients at intermediate risk for atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease (ASCVD) and those with family history of ASCVD. An overall genetic risk score (GRS) [LOINC: 93306-9] and interpretation (high/low) [LOINC: 93307-7] may be provided to improve risk estimates and aid in the prevention of CHD. The GRS is based on the genotype of multiple genetic variants associated with CHD. Additionally, a patient's overall 10-year probability of CHD may be calculated by multiply the 10-year ASCVD score from the Pooled Cohort Equations by the GRS.

[93308-5] Genetic variation's effect on risk of adverse drug reaction -- **Component:** Risk of adverse drug reaction sequence variation interpretation **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Nar  **Short name:** Adverse drug seq var interp Bld/T-Imp  **Definition:** The predicted risk of an adverse drug reaction based on the variant(s) detected. For example, a patient who is HLA-B*57:01 positive has a significant risk of abacavir hypersensitivity reactions (HSR). For individuals taking pazopanib and found to be HLA-B*57:01 carriers, they have an increased risk of elevated alanine aminotransferase (ALT) levels when taking pazopanib.

[93340-8] PhenX - complete blood count protocol 220501 -- **Component:** PhenX - complete blood count protocol 220501 **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** PhenX  **Short name:** PhenX - CBC protocol  **Definition:** The Complete Blood Count (CBC) protocol is a standard blood panel from the National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey (NHANES) and performed on participants aged 1 and older. Blood is collected from participants via standard venipuncture in a 3 or 4 ml K3 EDTA tube. The ambient blood sample is placed into a hematological analyzer for CBC and differential analyses. The blood should be tested as soon as possible after venipuncture and no later than 24 hours.

[93354-9] Neutrophil CD64 and Monocyte HLA DR panel - Blood by Flow cytometry (FC) -- **Component:** Neutrophil CD64 & Monocyte HLA DR panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Flow cytometry  **Short name:** Neut CD64 + Mono HLA DR Pnl Bld FC  **Definition:** This panel is intended to help distinguish between infectious and non-infectious systemic inflammatory response syndrome (SIRS) by assessing upregulation of CD64 on neutrophils.  Expression of HLA-DR on monocytes provides additional prognostic information.  Antibiotic treatment before sample collection may affect CD64 expression and will affect interpretation of the assay. Interferon-gamma, G-CSF, or other drugs that affect their levels, cause upregulation of CD64 expression on neutrophils and will affect interpretation of the assay.

[93477-8] Busulfan given [Mass] of Dose -- **Component:** Busulfan given **Property:** Mass **Time aspect:** XXX **System:** Dose **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Busulfan Gvn ?Tm Dose  **Definition:** Term is intended to represent the total mass of busulfan infused over a 2 hour period.
[93478-6] Busulfan area under the curve panel - Plasma -- **Component:** Busulfan area under the curve panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Stdy **System:** Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Busulfan AUC Pnl Plas  **Definition:** The results of this panel will help guide dosing adjustments to achieve complete bone marrow ablation while minimizing dose-dependent toxicity of busulfan.  Busulfan is generally given in 2H infusions, once every 6H for 4 days (a total of 16 doses).  Ideally, this pharmacokinetic evaluation of the area under the curve (AUC) and clearance after the first dose is performed to help guide dosing with results available by the 5th dose.
[93479-4] Observation interpretation -- **Component:** Observation interpretation **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Observation Imp  **Definition:** The overall interpretation (high/low, abnormal/normal, indeterminate/negative/positive, etc.) of the observation value. In some systems, the observation interpretation is being communicated in v2.x in OBX-8 (Observation Interpretation), in v3 in ObservationInterpretation (CWE) and in FHIR in Observation.interpretation.[https://www.hl7.org/fhir/valueset-observation-interpretation.html]
[93480-2] Cortisol post CRH stimulation panel - Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Cortisol post corticotropin releasing hormone stimulation panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Cortisol p CRH stimulation Pnl SerPl  **Definition:** The corticotropin releasing hormone (CRH) stimulation test is used to evaluate the hypothalamic-pituitary axis and help diagnose adrenocorticotrophic hormone (ACTH or corticotropin) dependent Cushing's disease. CRH stimulation cannot distinguish between Cushing's and pseudo-Cushing's. During this procedure, baseline cortisol measurements are taken followed by administration of 1 ug/kg of CRH, after which cortisol is measured again at timed intervals. In patients with Cushing's, cortisol concentrations at 30 and 45 minutes post CRH administration should be at least 20% higher than the mean baseline value.[NCBI Books: NBK278940] The timing for baseline and post-CRH cortisol measurements vary depending on local protocol. ACTH levels may also be measured [LOINC: 91707-0] as part of a CRH stimulation test.

[93690-6] BRAF gene V600 mutations [Identifier] in Plasma cell-free DNA by Molecular genetics method Nominal -- **Component:** BRAF gene.p.Val600 mutations **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Plas.cfDNA **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** BRAF p.V600 mutations Plas.cfDNA  **Definition:** Detection of BRAF V600 variants, including V600E (Val600Glu, c.1799T>A) and V600K (Val600Lys, c.1798_1799delGTinsAA), in cell-free DNA. Using cell-free DNA is alternative to invasive tissue biopsies. The most prevalent mutations are V600E (70−90%) and V600K (5−30%); other mutations are rare. A positive result indicates the presence of an activating BRAF mutation and may be useful for guiding the treatment of individuals with melanoma.

[93726-8] Dermatan sulfate and heparan sulfate and keratan sulfate panel - Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Dermatan sulfate & heparan sulfate & keratan sulfate panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** -  **Short name:** MPS panel SerPl  **Definition:** Mucopolysaccharidoses (MPSs) are rare genetic disorders of glycosaminoglycan (GAG) catabolism caused by a deficiency in the activity of a single, specific lysosomal enzyme required for GAG degradation. GAGs include dermatan sulfate, heparan sulfate, keratan sulfate, and chondroitin sulfate. MPS patients have elevated GAG fragments in blood, urine and cerebral spinal fluid (CSF) as well as a buildup of incompletely degraded GAG in lysomes. The GAG accumulation interferes with normal cell, tissue and organ function, which may result in cardiac abnormalities, organ failure, cognitive impairment, and skeletal or joint abnormalities.  There are nine primary MPS disorders, MPS I - MPS IX.  Each type is associated with a specific enzyme deficiency and gene locus.  Measuring GAG levels in urine can screen for MPS disorders, though false-negatives are common. To distinguish specific MPS disorders, enzyme activity assays based on cultured fibroblasts, leucocytes, plasma or serum are utilized. Predicting MPS disease severity is difficult for most types, however, patients who are homozygous for a nonsense allele or have two different nonsense alleles have the severe form of MPS I. [PMID: 22210669]

[93799-5] Chromosome 2 copy number/nucleus in Tissue by FISH -- **Component:** Chromosome 2 copy number/nucleus **Property:** EntNum **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tiss **Scale:** Qn **Method:** FISH  **Short name:** Chr 2 copy num/nucleus EntNum Tiss FISH  **Definition:** Aneusomy (varying number) of chromosome 2 is evaluated by using a DNA probe that marks the centromeric region of chromosome 2. [93800-1] 1p/1q chromosome deletion [# Ratio] in Tissue by FISH -- **Component:** 1p chromosome deletion/1q chromosome deletion **Property:** NRto **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tiss **Scale:** Qn **Method:** FISH  **Short name:** 1p/1q Chrom Del Tiss FISH  **Definition:** The ratio of chromosome 1p arm deletions to chromosome 1q arm deletions. Results are used to diagnose oligodendroglioma brain tumors and to determine best treatment options. Tumors with 1p/1q ratio <0.80 and ≥24% deleted cells are deemed deleted for 1p.
[93801-9] Chromosome 1 polysomy [Presence] in Tissue by FISH -- **Component:** Chromosome 1 polysomy **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tiss **Scale:** Ord **Method:** FISH  **Short name:** Chr 1 polysomy Tiss Ql FISH  **Definition:** This test determines if chromosome 1 polysomy is present in tissue (tumor) specimens. Chromosome 1 polysomy is defined as 3 or more signals for 1q and/or 19p in >30% of tumor cells with concurrent 1p/19q deletion.[PMID: 19808867] Chromosome 1 polysomy is a prognostic indicator of poorer outcome for oligodendroglioma patients.
[93802-7] 19q/19p chromosome deletion [# Ratio] in Tissue by FISH -- **Component:** 19q chromosome deletion/19p chromosome deletion **Property:** NRto **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tiss **Scale:** Qn **Method:** FISH  **Short name:** 19q/19p Chrom Del Tiss FISH  **Definition:** The ratio of chromosome 19q arm deletions to chromosome 19p arm deletions. Results are used to diagnose oligodendroglioma brain tumors and to determine best treatment options. Tumors with 19q/19p ratio <0.80 and ≥26% deleted cells are deemed deleted for 19q.
[93803-5] Chromosome 19 polysomy [Presence] in Tissue by FISH -- **Component:** Chromosome 19 polysomy **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tiss **Scale:** Ord **Method:** FISH  **Short name:** Chr 19 polysomy Tiss Ql FISH  **Definition:** This test determines if chromosome 19 polysomy is present in tissue (tumor) specimens. Polysomy is defined as 3 or more signals for 1q and/or 19p in >30% of tumor cells with concurrent 1p/19q deletion.[PMID: 19808867] Chromosome 19 polysomy is a prognostic indicator of poorer outcome for oligodendroglioma patients.
[93804-3] Chromosome 12 copy number/nucleus in Tissue by FISH -- **Component:** Chromosome 12 copy number/nucleus **Property:** EntNum **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tiss **Scale:** Qn **Method:** FISH  **Short name:** Chr 12 copy num/nucleus EntNum Tiss FISH  **Definition:** Aneusomy (varying number) of chromosome 12 is evaluated by using a DNA probe that marks the centromeric region of chromosome 12. The ratio of the number of signals of the MDM2 gene, also on chromosome 12, to the chromosome 12 signal [LOINC: 93809-2] can indicate whether or not there is amplification of the MDM2 gene.

[93905-8] ABO group [Type] in Blood--during infancy -- **Component:** ABO group^during infancy **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** ABO Group infant Bld  **Definition:** During the first 4-6 months of life reverse grouping on infants may reflect maternal antibodies and it may take several months for newborns to develop their own antibodies against the A and B antigens not present on their own cells.
