[94076-7] Mutations/Megabase [# Ratio] in Tumor -- **Component:** Mutations/Megabase **Property:** NRto **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tumor **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Muts/Mb Tumor  **Definition:** Tumor mutation/mutational burden (TMB), also known as mutation load, is a biomarker for various cancer types to identify patients who will benefit from immunotherapy, such as immune checkpoint blockade (ICB) therapy.[PMID: 30395155] Various quantitative and ordinal results may be reported, so it is important to distinguish the type of results when mapping to LOINC. Quantitative results typically include the number of mutations detected per megabase (mut/Mb), often by NGS, in a tumor specimen (this term). The mutations targeted may include non-synonymous or both synonymous and non-synonymous mutations. An ordinal TMB result [LOINC: 94077-5] of high, low, etc., may also be reported to provide an overall interpretation for the quantitative value (muts/Mb). We encourage labs to report how results are determined or calculated in either the structured message format or using another LOINC.
[94077-5] Tumor mutation burden [Interpretation] -- **Component:** Tumor mutation burden **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tumor **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** TMB Tumor-Imp  **Definition:** Tumor mutational burden (TMB), also known as mutation load, is a biomarker for various cancer types to identify patients who will benefit from immunotherapy, such as immune checkpoint blockade (ICB) therapy. Testing can be performed by whole exome sequencing (WES) or next-generation sequencing (NGS). Various quantitative and ordinal results may be reported, so it is important to distinguish the type of results when mapping to LOINC. Quantitative results typically include the number of mutations detected per megabase (mut/Mb), often by NGS, in a tumor specimen [LOINC: 94076-7]. The mutations targeted may include non-synonymous or both synonymous and non-synonymous mutations. Quantitative TMB results have also been reported as the total number of mutations detected by whole exome sequencing (WES). We encourage labs to report how results are determined or calculated in either the structured message format or using another LOINC. An ordinal TMB result (this term) of high, low, etc., may also be reported to provide an overall interpretation for the quantitative value (muts/Mb). Cut-offs vary across assays and for each tumor type, so it is important to know which assay was used to determine the overall ordinal TMB result.

[94096-5] Collagen type VII IgG Ab [Presence] in Serum or Plasma by Immunoassay -- **Component:** Collagen type VII Ab.IgG **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Ord **Method:** IA  **Short name:** CollagenVII IgG SerPl Ql IA  **Definition:** Detection of IgG antibodies against Collagen type VII in serum or plasma to assist in the diagnosis of epidermolysis bullosa acquisita (EBA). The term was created for, but not limited in use to, the submitter's test kit, Euroimmun Anti-Collagen type VII ELISA (IgG), which calculates the ratio of the extinction value of the control or patient sample over the extinction value of the calibrator. A negative serological result does not exclude the presence of disease.

[94122-9] Oxygen consumption (VO2)/Body weight [Volume Rate Content] --peak during exercise -- **Component:** Oxygen consumption/Body weight^peak during exercise **Property:** VRatCnt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** VO2/BW max dur exercise
[94123-7] Pulmonary artery End diastolic blood pressure by Cardiac catheterization -- **Component:** Intravascular end diastolic **Property:** Pres **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Pulmonary artery **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Cardiac catheterization  **Short name:** dPAP by Cardiac cath  **Definition:** Diastolic pulmonary artery pressure (dPAP) is used with pulmonary artery wedge pressure (PAWP) to help distinguish post-capillary pulmonary hypertension from combined post- and pre-capillary pulmonary hypertension associated with left heart failure. The diastolic pressure gradient (DPG) is calculated as dPAP minus PAWP, and is an alternate to the transpulmonary pressure gradient (TPG), which is calculated as the mean pulmonary artery pressure (mPAP) minus PAWP. An elevated DPG or TPG indicates that the increase in pulmonary pressure is not solely due to increased PAWP (post-capillary), but rather has a pre-capillary component as well, as reflected in an dPAP/mPAP increase out of proportion to PAWP. [PMID: 27587711]
[94124-5] Ventilatory threshold [Volume Rate Content] -- **Component:** Ventilatory threshold **Property:** VRatCnt **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Ventilatory threshold  **Definition:** The ventilatory threshold (VT) is the intensity of exercise at which the production of carbon dioxide and consequent increase in ventilation, both of which are related to the production of lactate during exercise, become disproportionately higher than oxygen consumption (VO2). The VT usually occurs at about 60-70% of maximum oxygen consumption. Both VT and maximum VO2 are predictors of mortality in patients with congestive heart failure. [PMID: 21977155] The specific method used to calculate the VT should be reported along with the result.

It is an alternative measurement to the slope of ventilation to carbon dioxide production as calculated for the duration of exercise. [94129-4] Water deprivation challenge panel - Urine and Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Water deprivation challenge panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Urine+Ser/Plas **Scale:** -  **Short name:** Water deprivation Pnl Ur+SerPl  **Definition:** A water deprivation test is used to help diagnose diabetes insipidus (DI) and differentiate between different forms of DI and other disorders such as primary polydipsia. In the first phase of the test, the body's response to fluid fasting is measured with a series of timed urine osmolality +/- serum/plasma analyte measurements. The exact analytes that are measured and the timing depends on the protocol used. At the end of the first phase, if the patient has not developed significant dehydration or hyperosmolarity (in which case the testing would be stopped), the second phase of testing is begun. The second phase involves assessing the body's response to a dose of vasopressin and helps differentiate between central and nephrogenic DI.

The PhenX Working Group modified the Adult Protocol to provide a more precise measure. The original wording of "fairly regularly" was changed to "every day." [94186-4] Origin of germline genetic variant [Type] -- **Component:** Origin of germline genetic variant **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Origin germline gen var  **Definition:** The source or inheritance of a specific germline variation found in an individual patient.  Understanding the source can help determine the significance of a particular variant and its association with different diseases or syndromes. Germline variants are either inherited or de novo. Inherited variants can be maternal, paternal, or a combination of maternal and paternal. De novo variants are those that are not present in parental germline cells, but that arise in the egg or sperm prior to conception or just after the zygote is formed. [PMID: 27894357]
[94187-2] Platelet aggregation [Interpretation] in Platelet rich plasma -- **Component:** Platelet aggregation **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** PRP **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** PA PRP-Imp  **Definition:** Abnormal platelet aggregation results typically are due to a medication effect (eg, aspirin or nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug use) and less commonly reflect an acquired or congenital platelet disorder. If present, acquired conditions include dysproteinemias, myeloproliferative diseases, and renal failure. Congenital conditions include Glanzmann thrombasthenia, Bernard-Soulier syndrome, Hermansky-Pudlak syndrome, storage pool disease, and Wiskott-Aldrich syndrome.
[94188-0] APC gene deletion+duplication and full mutation analysis in Blood or Tissue by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** APC gene deletion+duplication & full mutation analysis **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** APC gene Del+Dup + Full Mut Anl Bld/T  **Definition:** Full gene sequence analysis is performed to detect the presence of a mutation in all coding regions and intron/exon boundaries of the APC gene. In addition, deletion/duplication analysis, such as by array comparative genomic hybridization (aCGH), is used to test for the presence of large deletions or duplications in the APC gene. This test is used to confirm a diagnosis of familial adenomatous polyposis (FAP) for patients with clinical findings.

[94266-4] CD11a, CD11b, and CD18 phenotyping panel - Blood Qualitative -- **Component:** CD11a & CD11b & CD18 phenotyping panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** CD11a + CD11b + CD18 Pnl Bld  **Definition:** Flow cytometric immunophenotyping evaluation for presence or absence of the CD11/CD18 complex using monoclonal antibodies directed against the CD11 isoforms, CD11a and CD11b, and CD18 antigens. [94327-4] PhenX - human papillomavirus vaccine use protocol 100802 -- **Component:** PhenX - human papillomavirus vaccine use protocol 100802 **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** PhenX  **Short name:** PhenX - HPV vaccine use protocol  **Definition:** The interviewer asks the individual whether s/he ever received a human papillomavirus (HPV) vaccination. If the response is yes, the interviewer asks about number of shots received.    If the participant answers "yes" to question 1, the interviewer should complete the rest of the protocol. If the participant answers "no," then the protocol is deemed complete.    The PhenX Working Group notes that GARDASIL® is also known internationally as Silgard.    European Medicines Agency. (2009, September 25). Silgard European Public Assessment Report.  Waknine, Y. (2006, October 2). International approvals: Singulair and Gardasil/Silgard. Medscape Today.    Note: GARDASIL® by Merck & Co. is approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for use in females ages 9-26 and males ages 9-26.    Note: CERVARIX® is manufactured by GlaxoSmithKline Biologicals and approved by the FDA for use in females ages 10-25.

[94339-9] Myelopathy autoimmune Ab panel - Serum -- **Component:** Myelopathy autoimmune Ab panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** -  **Short name:** Myelopathy Ab pnl Ser  **Definition:** This panel contains results for the evaluation of patients with suspected autoimmune myelopathy, myelitis, or paraneoplastic myelopathy using serum specimens. Autoimmune myelopathy evaluation of both serum and CSF [LOINC: 94353-0] can assist in the diagnosis (e.g. paraneoplastic or idiopathic autoimmune) and distinguishing from other causes of myelopathy, such as multiple sclerosis, sarcoidosis, or vascular disease. Results are used in conjunction with clinical history, examinations, and/or MRIs of the spinal cord to determine an autoimmune diagnosis. Additionally, early testing may assist in earlier diagnosis of occult cancer and initiation of immune therapies.

[94397-7] Sulfocysteine, Hypoxanthine, Xanthine, Urate and Creatinine panel - Urine -- **Component:** Sulfocysteine, Hypoxanthine, Xanthine, Urate & Creatinine panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Urine **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** SSC HypoXan Xan Urate Pnl Ur  **Definition:** This panel contains quantitative results for s-sulfocysteine, xanthine, hypoxanthine and uric acid in urine specimens. Testing is performed for the diagnosis of or monitoring patients being treated for molybdenum cofactor deficiency (MoCD, also known as type III xanthinuria), isolated sulfite oxidase deficiency (ISOD) and hereditary xanthinuria. Urine s-sulfocysteine is elevated in both MoCD and ISOD, which have similar clinical phenotypes. Hypoxanthine and xanthine are increased in MoCD but normal in ISOD. Patients with xanthinuria have increased xanthine and decreased uric acid concentrations in urine.

[94429-8] HLA-DP and DQ and DR (class II) IgG low risk [Identifier] in Serum or Plasma by Flow cytometry (FC) -- **Component:** HLA-DP & DQ & DR (class II) Ab.IgG low risk **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Flow cytometry  **Short name:** HLA class II Ab.IgG low risk SerPl FC  **Definition:** Laboratories that perform HLA antibody testing often report the specific antibodies found in categories based on the "amount" of each antibody found (called mean fluorescent intensity, MFI, for the common Luminex bead method). Theoretically, the antibodies present in higher concentrations, which are more likely to react with donor antigens, will have higher MFI values. Since risk stratification based on MFI range is not standardized and the exact cutoffs vary from lab to lab, this term is intended to report those HLA antibodies found in the lower risk stratification of MFI values, but still detectable and possibly having a risk of rejection (For one lab this range was 500 < MFI < 1999).

[94433-0] HLA-DP and DQ and DR (class II) IgG with prozone effect [Identifier] in Serum or Plasma by Flow cytometry (FC) -- **Component:** HLA-DP & DQ & DR (class II) Ab.IgG with prozone effect **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Flow cytometry  **Short name:** HLA classII Ab.IgG prozone SerPl FC  **Definition:** Sometimes high titer antibody can be associated with low levels of detection (low mean fluorescence intensity, MFI, for the Luminex HLA bead assay), a phenomenon often referred to as the "prozone" effect. This term is intended to report those antibodies that were not detected at significant level with original sample, but became detectable after dilution or assay treatment.  Some laboratories refer to these "prozone antigens observed", meaning those antigens for which the patient's antibody exhibited prozone effect.
[94434-8] HLA-DPB1 IgG Ab [Identifier] in Serum or Plasma by Flow cytometry (FC) -- **Component:** HLA-DPB1 Ab.IgG **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Flow cytometry  **Short name:** HLA-DPB1 IgG SerPl FC  **Definition:** Some high specificity assays,such as the Luminex single bead system, detect epitopes at a detailed molecular allele-equivalent level.  Therefore, many laboratories using this assay will report, along with the serological type, the molecular type, and also the mean fluorescence intensity (MFI) in a single answer. This term is intended to cover the existing reporting formats for HLA-DPB1 antibody specificity.

However, some high specificity assays,such as the Luminex single bead system, can detect epitopes at a more detailed molecular allele-equivalent level.  Therefore, many laboratories using this assay will report, along with the serotype, the molecular type, and also the mean fluorescence intensity (MFI) in a single answer. This term is intended to cover the existing reporting formats for HLA-A antibody specificity.
[94446-2] HLA-A and B and C (class I) IgG panel - Serum or Plasma by Flow cytometry (FC) -- **Component:** HLA-A & B & C (class I) Ab.IgG panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** - **Method:** Flow cytometry  **Short name:** HLA-A + B + C (class I) IgG Pnl SerPl FC  **Definition:** This panel was created for, but not restricted in use to, the Labscan single antigen bead multianalyte HLA antibody system detected with Luminex Labscan flow analyzer.
[94447-0] HLA-DP and DQ and DR (class II) IgG high risk [Identifier] in Serum or Plasma by Flow cytometry (FC) -- **Component:** HLA-DP & DQ & DR (class II) Ab.IgG high risk **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Flow cytometry  **Short name:** HLA class II Ab.IgG high risk SerPl FC  **Definition:** Laboratories that perform HLA antibody testing often report the specific antibodies found in categories based on the "amount" of each antibody found (called mean fluorescent intensity, MFI, for the common Luminex bead method). Theoretically, the antibodies present in higher concentrations, which are more likely to react with donor antigens, will have higher MFI values. Since risk stratification based on MFI range is not standardized and the exact cutoffs vary from lab to lab, this term is intended to report those HLA antibodies found in the higher risk stratification of MFI values (For one lab this was MFI > 5000, but some laboratories use 10,000 or another value).

[94577-4] Maximum clot strength amplitude [Length] in Blood by Thromboelastography.resonance.arachidonate induced -- **Component:** Maximum clot strength amplitude **Property:** Len **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Thromboelastography.resonance.arachidonate induced  **Short name:** Maximum clot strength amplitude Bld Thromboelastography.resonance.arachidonate induced  **Definition:** The maximum clot strength in the presence of arachidonic acid, as measured by the amplitude of resonance of the blood meniscus as the blood sample clots. In this test, thrombin activity is blocked, and arachidonic acid (AA) is used to activate platelets in order to measure the clot strength based on platelet activation by AA, which result is used to determine the relative contribution of AA-induced platelet activation [LOINC: 94579-0] to the overall clot strength [LOINC: 91125-5]. This term was created for, but is not limited in use to, the Submitter's test kit, the TEG6s PlateletMapping ADP & AA Assay Cartridge for heparinized blood.
[94578-2] Clot strength [Length fraction] in Blood by Thromboelastography.platelet thromboxane A2 receptor inhibited -- **Component:** Clot strength **Property:** LenFr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Thromboelastography.platelet thromboxane A2 receptor inhibited  **Short name:** Clot strength TXA2-R inhib LenFr Bld  **Definition:** A calculated value representing the relative amount of clotting strength (i.e., platelet activity) that would remain in the presence of anti-platelet thromboxane A2 (TXA2)-receptor inhibiting drugs. It is the portion of clotting strength due to factors other than arachidonic acid, which activates platelets by a pathway that includes metabolism to thromboxane A2 (TXA2), which binds to the TXA2 receptor. This result is used to guide anti-platelet therapy decisions. For example, if a patient's baseline relative clotting strength due to AA activation is 25% [LOINC: 94579-0], then treating that patient with an anti-platelet TXA2 receptor inhibitor would only reduce clotting strength by 25%, leaving a predicted 75% intact.
[94579-0] Clot strength [Length fraction] in Blood by Thromboelastography.arachidonate induced -- **Component:** Clot strength **Property:** LenFr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Thromboelastography.arachidonate induced  **Short name:** Clot strength LenFr Bld TEG.arachidonate induced  **Definition:** Calculation of percentage of clot strength due to arachidonic acid (AA)-induced platelet aggregation.  This is calculated based on measurements of the patient's maximum clot strength and clot strength due to AA-induced platelet aggregation, via methods such as thromboelastography or resonance thromboelastography. This is the residual portion of the clot strength which is not inhibited, in response to arachidonate-induced activation of platelets. Used to guide anti-platelet therapy decisions.

[94586-5] Dermatan sulfate and heparan sulfate and keratan sulfate panel - DBS -- **Component:** Dermatan sulfate & heparan sulfate & keratan sulfate panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld.dot **Scale:** -  **Short name:** MPS panel DBS  **Definition:** This panel contains quantitative measurements of dermatan sulfate (DS), heparin sulfate (HS), and keratan sulfate (KS) in dried blood spots. Elevations of dermatan sulfate and/or heparan sulfate is associated with mucopolysaccharidoses types I, II, III, or VI. Elevations of keratan sulfate is associated with mucopolysaccharidoses type IV. Testing is performed to screen newborns for mucopolysaccharidosis (MPS) types I and II and to aid in the diagnosis and monitoring of patients with MPS types I, II, III, IV, and VI.
[94587-3] Germline disorder chromosome analysis in Amniotic fluid or Chorionic villus sample by Mate pair sequencing -- **Component:** Germline disorder chromosome analysis **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Amnio fld/CVS **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Mate pair sequencing  **Short name:** Germline chromo analy Amn/CVS MPSeq  **Definition:** This term is used for the order & overall report for chromosome analysis by sequencing methods, such as mate pair sequencing, for the detection of germline (congenital) chromosome abnormalities in amniotic fluid or chorionic villus sampling (CVS) specimens. Results may include the overall findings, the result in ISCN format, testing methods, interpretation, recommendations, and references. Testing is performed when previous cytogenetic studies are unsuccessful or detect a chromosome abnormality of unknown significance. Results provide diagnostic, prognostic, and therapeutic information for patient care.
[94588-1] Myeloid neoplasm chromosome analysis in Blood or Marrow by Mate pair sequencing -- **Component:** Myeloid neoplasm chromosome analysis **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Bone mar **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Mate pair sequencing  **Short name:** Myeloid neoplasm chr analy Bld/Mar MPSeq  **Definition:** This term is used for the order & overall chromosome analysis report for identification of neoplastic clones in blood or bone marrow specimens by mate pair sequencing in patient's with myeloid malignancies, such as acute myeloid leukemia (AML). Results may include the overall findings, the result in ISCN format, testing methods, interpretation, recommendations, and references. [94592-3] Chromosome rearrangement [Identifier] in Blood or Tissue by Mate pair sequencing Nominal -- **Component:** Chromosome rearrangement **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Mate pair sequencing  **Short name:** AML chromo analys Bld/Mar MPSeq  **Definition:** This term is used to report the mate pair sequencing results, preferably using a standardize nomenclature such as ISCN, for targeted chromosome analysis of neoplastic clones in blood or bone marrow specimens. [94593-1] Multiplex allergen panel - Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Multiplex allergen panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** -  **Short name:** Multiplex Allergy Pnl SerPl  **Definition:** This panel code contains results for a multiplex allergen assay for the detection and quantification of multiple allergies in a single assay. Child elements for this panel are not listed since results will vary, depending on the assay used. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the submitter's assay, ALEX Allergy Explorer2, a quantitative in vitro diagnostic test for the measurement of allergen specific IgE (sIgE) and a semi-quantitative in vitro diagnostic test for the measurement of total IgE (tIgE) in human serum or non-EDTA plasma. Similar multiplex allergen assays, such as the Immuno Solid-phase Allergen Chip (ISAC) (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Phadia, Sweden), can also be ordered and reported (overall study report) using this LOINC code. Both the ALEX Allergy Explorer and ISAC assays test for over 100 different allergens. LOINC codes specific for each allergen can be used to report the separate results provided within the multiplex allergen report.

[94691-3] Dermatan sulfate, heparan sulfate, chondroitin-6-sulfate, keratan sulfate panel - Urine -- **Component:** Dermatan sulfate & heparan sulfate & chondroitin-6-sulfate & keratan sulfate panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Urine **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** MPS panel Ur  **Definition:** This panel contains quantitative measurements of dermatan sulfate (DS), heparin sulfate (HS), keratan sulfate (KS), and chondroitin-6-sulfate (C6S) in urine. Elevations of dermatan sulfate and/or heparan sulfate is associated with mucopolysaccharidoses types I, II, III, VI and VII. Elevations of keratan sulfate is associated with mucopolysaccharidoses type IVA and IVB. C6S in urine is a marker for mucopolysaccharidoses IVA and VII. Testing is performed to aid in the diagnosis and monitoring of patients with mucopolysacchariodoses (MPS) types I, II, III, IV, VI, and VII.

[94753-1] Psychotropic medication pharmacogenomic analysis in Blood or Tissue by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** Psychotropic medication pharmacogenomic analysis **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** Psychotropic Pharmg Analy Bld/T  **Definition:** A comprehensive analysis for multiple genes that have strong pharmacogenomic associations with medications used in the treatment of psychiatric disorders, including depression. Each sample is tested for specific genetic variations with known functional impact. Depending on the lab, this test may include targeted analysis for following genes: ANKK1, ADRA2, CHRNA3, COMT, CYP1A2, CYP2B6, CYP2C9, CYP2C19, CYP2D6, CYP3A4, CYP3A5, DRD2, EPHX1, GRIK4, HLA-A (such as HLA-A*31:01 allele), HLA-B (such as HLA-B*15:02 allele), HTR2A, HTR2C, MTHFR, OPRM1, SCN1A, SLC6A4 (5-HTT), and UGT2B15. Duplications and multiplications of alleles may also be identified. Various genotyping assay methods, such as real-time PCR, sequencing, and deletion/duplication analysis, may be used.

[94818-2] Paraneoplastic Ab panel - Cerebral spinal fluid by Immunofluorescence -- **Component:** Paraneoplastic Ab panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** CSF **Scale:** - **Method:** IF  **Short name:** Paraneoplastic Ab Pnl CSF IF  **Definition:** This panel contains results for the detection of paraneoplastic antibodies in cerebral spinal fluid (CSF) specimens by immunofluorescence (IF). [94819-0] SARS-CoV-2 (COVID-19) RNA [Log #/volume] (viral load) in Specimen by NAA with probe detection -- **Component:** SARS coronavirus 2 RNA **Property:** LnCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Probe.amp.tar  **Short name:** SARS-CoV-2 RNA Spec NAA+probe-Log#  **Definition:** Quantitative detection of SARS coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) viral load in a specimen, such as a nasopharyngeal swab, blood, serum, or stool, by nucleic acid amplification with probe-based detection. The viral load reflects how well a virus is replicating in an infected person. A high viral load for SARS-CoV-2 indicates a significant amount of coronavirus is present in the patient.
[94820-8] Whole mRNA transcriptome sequence analysis in Blood or Tissue by Sequencing -- **Component:** Whole mRNA transcriptome sequence analysis **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Sequencing  **Short name:** Whole mRNA Seq Analysis Bld/T Seq  **Definition:** Whole mRNA transcriptome analysis by sequencing (RNA-Seq) is performed to determine what effect, if any, variants of unknown significance (VUS) may have on gene expression under specific circumstances or in a specific cell. Effect on the transcripts include a splice site defect, an inclusion of a pseudo-exon, exclusion of a real exon or loss of expression of the gene. Whole RNA transcriptome analysis is used in cancer diagnostics as well as other conditions to assess the functional impact of a variant on gene expression. While RNA-Seq studies looks at RNA transcribed from (mostly) exonic regions of DNA, whole exome sequencing studies [LOINC: 86205-2] look directly at the DNA contained in exonic regions of the genome. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the submitter's MNG Whole Transcriptome assay, which is used to assess all genes in mRNA that are expressed in the specimen and identify changes that could be associated with the patient's clinical presentation. It is recommended that RNA-Seq assays are performed in the context of DNA sequencing results since some variants may not be detected in RNA.

[94858-8] Glycerol [Moles/volume] in Serum by calculation -- **Component:** Glycerol **Property:** SCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated  **Short name:** Glycerol Ser Calc-sCnc  **Definition:** Glycerol concentration calculated based on the difference between triglyceride (TG) and glycerol-corrected TG measurements. Triglyceride is normally measured based on the amount of glycerol present after TG hydrolysis, but in certain conditions such as glycerol kinase deficiency, free glycerol is significantly elevated, which causes TG to appear falsely elevated. To account for this, free glycerol is removed prior to TG hydrolysis to get a true representation of the triglyceride measurement (glycerol-corrected TG) [LOINC: 94865-3].
[94859-6] Glycerol and glycerol-corrected triglyceride panel - Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Glycerol & Glycerol-corrected triglyceride panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** -  **Short name:** Glycerol + glycerol corr TG Pnl SerPl  **Definition:** This panel is used to evaluate triglyceride and glycerol levels in patients with suspected pseudohypertriglyceridemia due to glycerol kinase deficiency.

[94864-6] Transthyretin-associated familial amyloidosis panel - Blood -- **Component:** Transthyretin-associated familial amyloidosis panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** -  **Short name:** TTR-assoc familial amyloidosis Pnl Bld  **Definition:** This panel is used to help diagnose patients with transthyretin-associated familial amyloidosis. [94865-3] Triglyceride [Mass/volume] corrected for glycerol in Serum -- **Component:** Triglyceride^^corrected for glycerol **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Trigl Glycer cor Ser-mCnc  **Definition:** Used to differentiate true hypertriglyceridemia from pseudohypertriglyceridemia. Triglyceride is normally measured by hydrolyzing triglycerides and measuring the amount of glycerol, but in certain conditions such as glycerol kinase deficiency, free glycerol is significantly elevated, which causes triglyceride to appear falsely elevated. In this assay, free glycerol is removed prior to hydrolysis, so that the glycerol measurement after hydrolysis is a true representation of the triglyceride measurement.

[95053-5] DOPamine [Mass/volume] in Plasma --supine -- **Component:** DOPamine^supine **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** DOPamine sup Plas-mCnc  **Definition:** The goal of the postural panels is to find out if the adrenal gland is sensing the posture change and secreting increased levels of catecholamines appropriately during the transition from supine to standing. This can help to diagnose syndromes of inherited and acquired autonomic dysfunction or autonomic neuropathies which may show insufficient release of catecholamines.   On the other end of the spectrum are pheochromocytomas and various neuroectodermal tumors which exhibit unregulated release of one or more catecholamines.

[95068-3] ADRB2 gene targeted mutation analysis in Blood or Tissue by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** ADRB2 gene targeted mutation analysis **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** ADRB2 Mut Anl Bld/T  **Definition:** This test detects polymorphisms, such as Gly16Arg (rs1042713) and Gln27Glu (rs1042714), in the beta-2 adrenergic receptor (ADRB2) gene. [95069-1] Chromosome 1q and 9 and 11 and 15 aneuploidy and 13q deletion in Bone marrow by FISH -- **Component:** Chromosome 1q & 9 & 11 & 15 aneuploidy & chromosome region 13q deletion **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bone mar **Scale:** Doc **Method:** FISH  **Short name:** Chr 1q+9+11+15 aneu 13q Del Mar FISH  **Definition:** The detection by FISH analysis of gains (duplications, aneuploidy) and losses (deletion or monosomy) of chromosome regions (e.g. 1q, 13q, 9, 11 and 15) associated with multiple myeloma. This study is performed to determine the prognostic risk for disease outcome. For example, gains of chromosome 1q and monosomy or deletions of 13q are associated with less favorable outcomes whereas gains of chromosome 9, 11, and 15 are associated with more favorable outcomes.
[95070-9] Chromosome 9 and 11 and 15 aneuploidy in Bone marrow by FISH -- **Component:** Chromosome 9 & 11 & 15 aneuploidy **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bone mar **Scale:** Doc **Method:** FISH  **Short name:** Chr 9+11+15 aneu Mar FISH  **Definition:** Analysis of chromosomes 9, 11 and 15 in bone marrow specimens using FISH probes (CEP9, CEP11 and CEP15) targeting the centromere region. Hyperdiploidy with gains in these chromosomes is associated with a more favorable outcome in myeloma patients.
[95071-7] Chromosome region 14q32 rearrangements in Bone marrow by FISH -- **Component:** Chromosome region 14q32 rearrangements **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bone mar **Scale:** Doc **Method:** FISH  **Short name:** Chr 14q32 Rearr Mar FISH  **Definition:** This tests detects 14q32 rearrangements by fluorescence in situ hybridization (FISH) in patients with multiple myeloma, a white blood cell cancer that affects plasma cells. Four common 14q32 translocations involving the IGH gene include CCND1/t(11;14), FGFR3/t(4;14), MAF/t(14;16) and MAFB/t(14;20).
[95072-5] 1,3 beta glucan [Mass/volume] in Cerebral spinal fluid -- **Component:** 1,3 beta glucan **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** CSF **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** 1,3 beta glucan CSF-mCnc  **Definition:** Quantitative measurement of (1-3)-beta-D-Glucan in the cerebral spinal fluid (CSF) of patients with symptoms or predisposing conditions for invasive fungal infection. (1-3)-beta-D-Glucan is an abundant cell wall polysaccharide found in most fungi. Testing is performed as diagnostic and prognostic aid of central nervous system (CNS) fungal infection and to evaluate for fungal meningitis associated with exposure to contaminated epidural steroid injections.[PMID: 25378578][PMID: 23363831]

[95121-0] Coagulation factor VIII activity and inhibitor panel - Platelet poor plasma by Chromogenic method -- **Component:** Coagulation factor VIII activity & inhibitor panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** PPP **Scale:** - **Method:** Chromo  **Short name:** Fact VIII Act + Inhib Pnl PPP Chro  **Definition:** This panel contains results for factor VIII activity levels and the presence inhibitors directed against coagulation factor VIII. Factor VIII inhibitors are IgG antibodies that when present can lead to the development of possibly life-threatening hemorrhages. These antibodies most commonly are found in patient with Hemophilia A, elderly patients, postpartum mothers, and autoimmune deficient patients.

[95123-6] Gene fusion transcript details in Blood or Tissue by Molecular genetics method Narrative -- **Component:** Gene fusion transcript details **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Nar **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** Gene fusion details Bld/T  **Definition:** Narrative information (unstructured) about the gene fusion transcript identified. [95124-4] Sarcoma targeted gene fusion transcript analysis in Tissue by Sequencing -- **Component:** Sarcoma targeted gene fusion transcript analysis **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Tiss **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Sequencing  **Short name:** Sarcoma gene fusion analysis Tiss Seq  **Definition:** Evaluation of multiple gene fusion transcripts by sequence analysis, such as by next-generation sequencing, to detect the presence of somatic sarcoma-related gene fusions in a tissue specimen. Testing is performed for the diagnosis of specific soft tissue and bone tumors (sarcomas) based on the detected gene fusions. For example, a fusion between PAX3 and FOXO1 genes indicates an alveolar rhabdomyosarcoma and the EWSR1-FLI1 gene fusion is a present in Ewing's sarcomas. The overall result summary (fusion detected/not detected) along with information about the fusions identified, fusions targeted/tested, interpretation, testing method(s) and recommendations are typically included in the report.

[95130-1] Aggressive prostate cancer risk [Score] by Calculated by ExoDx -- **Component:** Aggressive prostate cancer risk **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated.ExoDx  **Short name:** Aggr PC risk Scre Patient Calc.ExoDx  **Definition:** This term was developed for ExoDx™ Prostate Test (IntelliScore). The ExoDx™ Prostate Test is a reverse-transcription PCR analysis of the genes PCA3, ERG, and SPDEF in urine.  The ExoDx's IntelliScore is calculated based on a proprietary algorithm that uses PCA3, ERG, and SPDEF  molecular results.  The score is used to surmise the patient's risk of aggressive prostate cancer and help determine whether or not a biopsy should be performed. [PMID: 30129068] The ExoDx™ Prostate Test differentiates high and low grade cancer from benign diseases, using a cutoff score of 15.6.  Patients with an Intelliscore above 15.6 are recommended for biopsy. [PMID: 27032035]

[95154-1] PRRSV ORF5 gene sequence homology to Prime Pac in Isolate by Sequencing -- **Component:** Porcine reproductive and respiratory syndrome virus ORF5 gene sequence homology to Prime Pac **Property:** NFr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Isolate **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Sequencing  **Short name:** PRRSV Prime Pac Hmlgy Islt Seq-NFr  **Definition:** Percent genomic homology between a new isolated PRRS virus strain and a PPRS vaccine strain (e.g. Lelystad, Fostera, IngelvacMLV, IngelvacATP, PrimePac and Prevacent) is based on the number of matching amino acids per total amino acids sequenced in the open reading frame 5 (ORF5) gene. The more homologous the ORF5 gene sequences, the more closely the variants are related. It is thought that sequence homology between isolates and vaccines will predict vaccine efficacy, however this is currently debated since little is known about the immune response to PRRS virus.

[95184-8] Cytomegalovirus T-cell immunodeficiency panel - Blood by Flow cytometry (FC) -- **Component:** Cytomegalovirus T-cell immunodeficiency panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Flow cytometry  **Short name:** CMV T-cell panel Bld FC  **Definition:** This panel contains results for Cytomegalovirus (CMV) CD8 T-Cell immune competence by Flow Cytometry. Patients tested are cytomegalovirus (CMV)-seropositive and may have 1 or more of the 5 major histocompatibility complex (MHC) alleles, including HLA A1, A2, B7, B8, or B35. Testing is performed to monitor CMV-specific CD8 T-cell responses in patients at higher risk of CMV disease. Testing may be performed on allo-hematopoietic stem cell or solid organ transplant patients who are at risk for developing late CMV disease or having CMV reactivation post transplant or after the cessation of antiviral prophylaxis. For transplant recipients and patients with autoimmune disorders receiving therapy with immunosuppressant agents, testing is useful for determining over immunosuppression within the CD8 T cell pathway. This panel was created for, but not limited in use to, Mayo's CMV CD8 T-Cell Immune Competence Quantitative Assessment by Flow Cytometry.[https://www.mayocliniclabs.com/test-catalog/Overview/88826]

[95290-3] Sphere measurement increment Refractometer -- **Component:** Sphere measurement increment **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Refractometer **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Sph incr RM  **Definition:** Sphere measurement increment is the specificity to which a refractometer can measure the sphere diopter, typically 0.25 diopters, but may be as small as 0.01 diopters. For example, a 0.25 diopter increment allows measurement of 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0 diopters, etc., while a 0.01 diopter increment allows finer measurement to a hundredth of a diopter.

[95410-7] SARS-CoV-2 (COVID-19) neutralizing antibody [Titer] in Serum by pVNT -- **Component:** SARS coronavirus 2 Ab.neut **Property:** Titr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** SemiQn **Method:** pVNT  **Short name:** SARS-CoV-2 NAb Titr Ser pVNT  **Definition:** Quantitative titer results for SARS Coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) virus neutralizing antibodies in serum by pVNT (pseudovirus neutralization test). Neutralizing antibody testing is performed to determine whether the antibodies produced after SARS-CoV-2 infection or in response to a vaccination are able to neutralize the virus and prevent infection or re-infection. Different methods can be used to determine the presence of neutralizing antibodies, including VNT (virus neutralization test) and pVNT, which are represented in LOINC as the Method "Neutralization" and "pVNT", respectively. The pVNT method for SARS-CoV-2 neutralizing antibody testing uses recombinant pseudoviruses that incorporate the S protein from SARS-CoV-2, whereas VNT uses SARS-CoV-2 virus from a clinical isolate or recombinant SARS-CoV-2 expressing reporter proteins.
[95411-5] SARS-CoV-2 (COVID-19) neutralizing antibody [Presence] in Serum by pVNT -- **Component:** SARS coronavirus 2 Ab.neut **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Ord **Method:** pVNT  **Short name:** SARS-CoV-2 NAb Ser Ql pVNT  **Definition:** Qualitative results for SARS Coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) virus neutralizing antibodies in serum by pVNT (pseudovirus neutralization test). Neutralizing antibody testing is performed to determine whether the antibodies produced after SARS-CoV-2 infection or in response to a vaccination are able to neutralize the virus and prevent infection or re-infection. Different methods can be used to determine the presence of neutralizing antibodies, including VNT (virus neutralization test) and pVNT, which are represented in LOINC as the Method "Neutralization" and "pVNT", respectively. The pVNT method for SARS-CoV-2 neutralizing antibody testing uses recombinant pseudoviruses that incorporate the S protein from SARS-CoV-2, whereas VNT uses SARS-CoV-2 virus from a clinical isolate or recombinant SARS-CoV-2 expressing reporter proteins.
[95412-3] COVID-19 case report -- **Component:** COVID-19 case report **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** {Setting} **Scale:** Doc **Method:** {Role}  **Short name:** COVID-19 Case rprt  **Definition:** This term represents a collection of information that would be reported to public health authorities about a patient who has or potentially has been exposed to SARS Coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) and may have or is diagnosed with having Coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19). This term does not represent a clinical COVID-19 case report that might be published in the medical literature or elsewhere. The term was created for, but not limited in use to, the CDC Human Infection with 2019 Novel Coronavirus Case Report Form [https://www.cdc.gov/coronavirus/2019-ncov/downloads/pui-form.pdf], and can be used for other COVID-19 case report forms, such as the WHO Revised case report form for Confirmed Novel Coronavirus COVID-19 [https://www.who.int/docs/default-source/coronaviruse/2019-covid-crf-v6.pdf?sfvrsn=c5ff90c6_2].

[95515-3] Angiotensin II (1-8) and angiotensin (1-7) panel - Plasma -- **Component:** Angiotensin II & angiotensin (1-7) panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Plas **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Ang II and Ang-(1-7) Pnl Plas  **Definition:** This panel contains quantitative results for angiotensin II (1-8) ("Ang II") and angiotensin (1-7) ("Ang 1-7") in plasma. Measurement of Ang II and Ang 1-7, which have opposing effects, is performed to provide a better estimation of the biological effects of activation of the renin-angiotensin-system. Testing may be performed as an adjunct to plasma renin activity (PRA) and aldosterone measurements for the diagnosis of primary aldosteronism and renal-vascular hypertension, and to investigate potential associations between Ang II, Ang 1-7, and outcomes of COVID-19.

[95546-8] Diabetes 8Y risk [Likelihood] Calculation -- **Component:** Diabetes 8Y risk **Property:** Likelihood **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculation  **Short name:** Diabetes 8Y risk Calcn  **Definition:** A patient's 8 year risk estimate for diabetes indicates the likelihood of developing Type 2 diabetes and can be calculated using a combination of life-style and health factors [PMID:12610029] or analysis on serum or plasma, such as lipidomic profiling [PMID:28733174]. [95548-4] Cardiovascular disease 10Y risk goal based on ACC-AHA Pooled Cohort by Goff 2013 -- **Component:** Cardiovascular disease 10Y risk goal **Property:** Likelihood **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** ACC-AHA Pooled Cohort.Goff 2013  **Short name:** CVD 10Y risk goal ACC-AHA.Goff 2013  **Definition:** This term represents the desired target for the patient's 10-year risk that would help prevent atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease (ASCVD) based on the Pooled Cohort Equations as published in the 2013 ACC/AHA Guideline on the Assessment of Cardiovascular Risk.[PMID: 24222018] The 10-year ASCVD risk goal estimates are based on the patient's age, sex, ethnicity and optimal levels for other risk factors, such as cholesterol levels, blood pressure, diabetes status, and smoking status.
[95549-2] Cardiovascular disease lifetime risk [Likelihood] ACC-AHA Pooled Cohort by Goff 2013 -- **Component:** Cardiovascular disease lifetime risk **Property:** Likelihood **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** ACC-AHA Pooled Cohort.Goff 2013  **Short name:** CVD Lifetime risk ACC-AHA.Goff 2013  **Definition:** The quantitative estimate for a patient's lifetime risk for having an atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease (ASCVD) based on the Pooled Cohort Equations as published in the 2013 ACC/AHA Guideline on the Assessment of Cardiovascular Risk. The lifetime ASCVD risk estimate is used an adjunct to short-term (10-year) risk assessment and to facilitate discussion regarding risk reduction through lifestyle change. It is calculated using the patient's age, sex, race, total cholesterol, HDL cholesterol, systolic blood pressure, blood pressure lowering medication use, diabetes status, and smoking status. The equations were developed by the ACC/AHA Risk Assessment Work Group and derived from non-Hispanic white and African American populations from 40 to 79 years of age. Per ACC/AHA, the estimates of lifetime risk are most directly applicable to non-Hispanic whites. They recommend using the values for other race/ethnic groups, but the estimates may represent under- and overestimates for persons of various ethnic groups.[PMID: 24222018] A spreadsheet enabling estimation of 10-year and lifetime risk for ASCVD is available for download at http://my.americanheart.org/cvriskcalculator.
[955-5] C sup(w) Ab [Presence] in Serum or Plasma from Blood product unit -- **Component:** C super little w Ab **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas^BPU **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** C sup(w) Ab SerPl BPU Ql
[95550-0] ABL1 gene c.944C>T [Presence] in Blood or Marrow by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** ABL1 gene.c.944C>T **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Bone mar **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** ABL1 c.944C>T Bld/Mar Ql  **Definition:** Detection of the single-point mutation, T315I, in the ABL1 gene within the BCR-ABL1 fusion transcript. Early detection of T315I mutation in patient's with chronic myeloid leukemia (CML) could inform alternative treatment before resistance is detected cytogenetically or before disease progression become evident. Mutations in the kinase domain of the BCR-ABL1 fusion are the leading cause of acquired imatinib resistance. Although mutations have been identified in more than 30 different amino acids, the highest degree of resistance is found to be associated with the T315I mutation.
[95551-8] Chromosome region 17p11.2 deletion in Blood or Tissue by FISH -- **Component:** Chromosome region 17p11.2 deletion **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss **Scale:** Doc **Method:** FISH  **Short name:** Chr 17p11.2 Del Bld/T FISH  **Definition:** FISH studies are performed using a probe targeting the p11.2 region on chromosome 17 to detect a deletion consistent with a diagnosis of 17p11.2 deletion (Smith-Magenis) syndrome. Additional signals are consistent with a diagnosis of 17p11.2 duplication (Potocki-Lupski) syndrome. LOINC codes with a Scale of "Doc" can be used for the order and to represent the overall report and collection of results (clinical indication, method, overall findings, ISCN result, interpretation, etc.).

FISH signal pattern indicating a loss of 4p16.3 is consistent with a diagnosis of WHS. [95619-3] Hurts, insults, threatens, and screams [HITS] -- **Component:** Hurts, insults, threatens, and screams **Property:** - **Time aspect:** 12Mo **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** HITS  **Definition:** The Hurt-Insult-Threaten-Scream (HITS) screening tool was developed as a quick, four question, domestic violence screen. Each response has a corresponding numeric value, ranging from 1(rarely) to 5 (frequently).  The four individual values are summed for a total score between 4 and 20. A cumulative score greater than 10 indicates the possibility of domestic partner abuse. [PMID: 24652635]
[95620-1] Neonatal pain, agitation, and sedation scale panel NPASS -- **Component:** Neonatal pain, agitation, & sedation scale panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** NPASS  **Short name:** NPASS pnl  **Definition:** The Neonatal Pain, Agitation, and Sedation Scale (N-PASS) is used to measure behaviors and physiologic signs that indicate pain and/or agitation, and sedation in newborns. The N-PASS is comprised of two separate scales: Pain and/or Agitation, and Sedation. Pain and agitation are difficult to differentiate in the neonate as the behaviors and physiologic signs are similar. Pain should be assessed regularly, appropriate to the situation. The Sedation Scale may not be required with every Pain/Agitation assessment; regular sedation assessment is indicated with the administration of potentially sedating medications. The assessment scores are used together to inform pain and sedation management therapy.
[95621-9] Pain/agitation panel NPASS -- **Component:** Pain &or agitation panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** NPASS  **Short name:** Pain/agitat pnl NPASS  **Definition:** The Pain/Agitation Scale measures the behaviors and physiologic signs associated with pain, which is difficult to differentiate from other sources of agitation in neonates.  Pain is scored from zero to positive two for each behavioral and physiologic criteria, then summed as a positive score.  Total score ranges from zero to ten in neonates greater than/equal to 30 weeks gestation.  One point is added to the total when the neonate is less than thirty weeks gestational age, with a total score range from one to eleven in the premature infant.

[95629-2] Sedation panel NPASS -- **Component:** Sedation panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** NPASS  **Short name:** Sed pnl NPASS  **Definition:** The Sedation Scale measures the behaviors and physiologic signs associated with sedation in the neonate.  Sedation level is scored from negative two to zero for each behavioral and physiologic criteria, then summed and noted as a negative score.  Sedation is mainly assessed during routine care or procedures, as most items assess response to stimulation. Note that a Sedation Scale negative score without administration of opioids/sedatives could indicate prolonged pain or stress response in the premature infant, or neurologic depression, sepsis, or other pathology in the neonate.

[95715-9] Population group -- **Component:** Population group **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Population group  **Definition:** Population groups are based on various characteristics, such as occupation, comorbidities, or age. Examples include public health workers, first respondents, pregnant women, children 3-18 with high-risk conditions, and adults 65 years or older. Population group assignments can be used for different purposes. For example, different population groups may be defined by individual jurisdictions (federal, state, or local) as having priority for receiving a vaccine in response to a public health event.

[95793-6] Immunization priority tier -- **Component:** Immunization priority tier **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Immunization priority tier  **Definition:** The vaccination priority tier depends on the severity of an infectious disease outbreak as well as the population group that a patient belongs to. For any given severity of outbreak or pandemic, a single population group falls into a single tier. So for example, in a low or moderate severity pandemic, a child between the ages of 3-18 without high risk conditions might be in tier 2 for vaccination priority, but as the pandemic worsens, they may be re-assigned to tier 3.

[95808-2] Cholesterol and triglyceride and chylomicrons panel - Urine -- **Component:** Cholesterol & triglyceride & chylomicrons panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Urine **Scale:** -  **Short name:** Cholest+triglyceride+chylomicrons Pnl Ur  **Definition:** This panel contains results for evaluation of cholesterol, triglycerides, and chylomicrons in urine to evaluate the fat content and aid in the diagnosis of chyluria (galacturia or milky urine), a condition in which chyle is present in the urine. Chyluria can have parasitic and non-parasitic causes. Parasitic chyluria is most prevalent in South East Asian countries where it is caused by parasitic infections spread by mosquitoes. Non-parasitic chyluria is most commonly due to impaired flow of lymph fluid due to a variety of reasons such as pregnancy, surgical procedures, tumors, lymphangiomas, and granulomatous infections.[PMID: 28478046][PMID: PMID: 26019826]

[95851-2] Somatotropin post macimorelin stimulation panel - Serum by Immunoassay -- **Component:** Somatotropin post macimorelin stimulation panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** - **Method:** IA  **Short name:** GH p Macimorelin Stil Pnl Ser IA  **Definition:** The macimorelin stimulation test is used to diagnose adult growth hormone (GH) deficiency. Macimorelin is a synthetic agonist of the ghrelin receptor that, when administered orally, stimulates growth hormone release from the pituitary in a dose-dependent manner, achieving maximum blood levels within 1 hour [PMID:23559086]. Serum GH concentrations falling below a set threshold during the timed assay following macimorelin administration confirms the presence of GH deficiency.

[95975-9] Skindex-16 [Skindex-16] -- **Component:** Skindex-16 **Property:** - **Time aspect:** 1W **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** Skindex-16  **Definition:** The Skindex-16 is a patient reported outcome instrument designed to asses how bothersome the effects of skin disease are in three areas of patient quality of life, symptoms, emotions and functioning. The Skindex-16 is general in nature and can be used for patients regardless of specific skin disease. Using on a Likert scale of 0 (Never) to 6 (Always), the patient rates how often of each skin disease effect has been over the preceding week. Skindex-16 provides a single score for each area (symptoms, emotions and functioning) as well as an average score. [PMID: 22284137]
[9597-6] Borrelia burgdorferi 93kD IgG Ab [Presence] in Serum by Immunoblot -- **Component:** Borrelia burgdorferi 93kD Ab.IgG **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Ord **Method:** IB  **Short name:** B burgdor93kD IgG Ser Ql IB
[95976-7] Symptom score [Skindex-16] -- **Component:** Symptom score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** 1W **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Skindex-16  **Definition:** Skindex Symptom Scores combine the responses to questions concerning itching, burning, pain, irritation and bleeding, which are then converted to a linear scale of 0 to 100. Each scale score for a given domain (Symptoms, Emotions, or Functioning) represents the average of the patient's responses to questions for that domain. Higher scores are associated with lower quality of life. [PMID: 22284137]
[95977-5] Emotion score [Skindex-16] -- **Component:** Emotion score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** 1W **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Skindex-16  **Definition:** Skindex Emotion Scores combine the responses to questions concerning worry, embarrassment, shame, frustration and depression, which are then converted to a linear scale of 0 to 100. Each scale score for a given domain (Symptoms, Emotions, or Functioning) represents the average of the patient's responses to questions for that domain. Higher scores are associated with lower quality of life. [PMID: 22284137]
[95978-3] Function score [Skindex-16] -- **Component:** Function score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** 1W **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Skindex-16  **Definition:** Skindex Functioning Scores combine the responses to questions concerning affects on relationships, staying home, work, and sleep, which are then converted to a linear scale of 0 to 100. Each scale score for a given domain (Symptoms, Emotions, or Functioning) represents the average of the patient's responses to questions for that domain. Higher scores are associated with lower quality of life. [PMID: 22284137]
[95979-1] Total score [Skindex-16] -- **Component:** Total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** 1W **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Skindex-16  **Definition:** Skindex Total Scores represent an average of the responses to all questions asked across the three domains, Symptom, Emotion and Function. The scores are summed, multiplied by 16.667, then divided by the number of questions asked to present scores in a linear (0-100) scale. As with individual domain scores, higher total scores are associated with lower quality of life.

[95996-5] Skindex-29 [Skindex-29] -- **Component:** Skindex-29 **Property:** - **Time aspect:** 4W **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** Skindex-29  **Definition:** The Skindex-29 is a patient reported outcome instrument designed to assess the effects of skin disease in three areas of patient quality of life, symptoms, emotions and functioning. The Skindex-29 is general in nature and can be used for patients regardless of specific skin disease. Using on a Likert scale of 1 (Never) to 5 (All the time), the patient rates the frequency of each skin disease effect over the preceding four weeks. The Skindex-29 is comprehensive and helpful in understanding the effects of a specific condition, and its wide use in clinical research allows for data comparison. Skindex-29 provides a single score for each area (symptoms, emotions and functioning) as well as an average score. [PMID: 22284137]
[95997-3] Symptom score [Skindex-29] -- **Component:** Symptom score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** 4W **System:** ^Event **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Skindex-29  **Definition:** Skindex Symptom Scores combine the responses to questions concerning itching, burning, pain, irritation and bleeding, which are then converted to a linear scale of 0 to 100. Each scale score for a given domain (Symptoms, Emotions, or Functioning) represents the average of the patient's responses to questions for that domain. Higher scores are associated with lower quality of life. [PMID: 22284137]
[95998-1] Emotion score [Skindex-29] -- **Component:** Emotion score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** 4W **System:** ^Event **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Skindex-29  **Definition:** Skindex Emotion Scores combine the responses to questions concerning worry, embarrassment, shame, frustration and depression, which are then converted to a linear scale of 0 to 100. Each scale score for a given domain (Symptoms, Emotions, or Functioning) represents the average of the patient's responses to questions for that domain. Higher scores are associated with lower quality of life. [PMID: 22284137]
[95999-9] Function score [Skindex-29] -- **Component:** Function score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** 4W **System:** ^Event **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Skindex-29  **Definition:** Skindex Functioning Scores combine the responses to questions concerning affects on relationships, staying home, work, and sleep, which are then converted to a linear scale of 0 to 100. Each scale score for a given domain (Symptoms, Emotions, or Functioning) represents the average of the patient's responses to questions for that domain. Higher scores are associated with lower quality of life. [PMID: 22284137]
[96000-5] Total score [Skindex-29] -- **Component:** Total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** 4W **System:** ^Event **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Skindex-29  **Definition:** Skindex Total Scores represent an average of the responses to all questions asked across the three domains, Symptom, Emotion and Function. The scores are summed, multiplied by 25, then divided by the number of questions asked to present scores in a linear (0-100) scale. As with individual domain scores, higher total scores are associated with lower quality of life.

[96034-4] Skindex mini [Skindex Mini] -- **Component:** Skindex mini **Property:** - **Time aspect:** 1W **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** Skindex mini  **Definition:** The Skindex mini is a patient reported outcome instrument that evaluates the effects of skin disease in three areas of patient quality of life: symptoms, emotions and functioning. It can be used to assess quality of life impact, regardless of specific skin disease. Skindex mini is similar to the Skindex-16, but has just three questions, one for each domain. While its brevity reduces the level of detail available in the Skindex-16, it is a fast and reliable means of gauging the impact of skin disease on patient quality of life. [PMID: 30590072]

[96106-0] University of Texas diabetic wound classification system panel Foot University of Texas diabetic wound classification system -- **Component:** University of Texas diabetic wound classification system panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Foot **Scale:** - **Method:** UT-DWCS  **Short name:** UT-DWCS pnl Ft UT-DWCS  **Definition:** The University of Texas diabetic wound classification system was designed to offer multidisciplinary teams a way to easily communicate the severity of diabetic foot wounds. The system grades wounds by depth (grade 0: pre- or postulcerative to grade 3: wounds penetrating bone or joints) and stage (stage A: clean to stage D: infected ischemic). The simple classification can be used to assess and monitor diabetic foot wounds in an effort to reduce amputations. [PMID:  8986890]

[96115-1] Level of tumor invasion [Type] in Colorectal cancer specimen by Haggitt -- **Component:** Level of tumor invasion **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Colorectal cancer specimen **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Haggitt  **Short name:** Tumor invasion level CRC spec Haggitt  **Definition:** The Haggitt classification for tumor invasion is a 0-4 level system developed to assess depth of invasion in pedunculated polyps compared to sessile polyps. In level 0, the carcinoma is confined to the mucosa. Levels 1 through 3 represent varying degrees of invasion into the polyp stalk. Level 4 represents invasion into the submucosa. All sessile malignant polyps are level 4 by definition.[PMID: 4007423]
[96116-9] Anterior surgical resection [Type] in Lower GI tract -- **Component:** Anterior surgical resection **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Lower GI tract **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** Anterior surg resection Lower GI tract  **Definition:** The type (e.g. high, low, ultralow) of anterior surgical resection procedure performed. In patients with low rectal cancers, the ultralow anterior resection (uLAR) may be performed. Post surgery, these patients often experience major bowel dysfunctions that affect their quality of life.[PMID: 29990965]

[96258-9] Cholesterol in LDL [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Calculated by Martin-Hopkins -- **Component:** Cholesterol.in LDL **Property:** SCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated.Martin-Hopkins  **Short name:** LDLc SerPl Mart-Hopk-sCnc  **Definition:** The Martin-Hopkins method for calculating LDL cholesterol uses a variable value for the triglyceride adjustment factor, where the adjustment factor depends on the measured triglyceride and non-HDL cholesterol levels. The calculation used since the 1970s, called the Friedewald formula, uses a fixed adjustment factor of 5, which results in an inaccurate calculated LDL result when triglycerides are high or LDL-C levels are low. [PMID: 24240933]

[9631-3] Viscosity of Semen -- **Component:** Viscosity **Property:** Visc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Semen **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Visc Smn  **Definition:** Viscosity is the measurement of resistance of a liquid to flow. Viscosity is a characteristic property of a fluid and typically is a constant for a given liquid at a given temperature. [PMID: 16830959] Semen may be measured quantitatively using a viscometer rather than the traditional subjective rating method. Determining the seminal viscosity with a quantitative method may be valuable in identifying and treating the subgroup of infertile men with viscid semen. [PMID: 1358311]

[96414-8] Pancreatic polypeptide sham feeding panel - Serum or Plasma -- **Component:** Pancreatic polypeptide sham feeding panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** -  **Short name:** PP Sham Feed Pnl SerPl  **Definition:** Pancreatic polypeptide (PP) is normally secreted by the pancreas in response to hypoglycemia, ingestion of food or chewing.  Sham feeding, where food is chewed but not swallowed, results in PP secretion due to vagal nerve stimulation, with a normal response to sham feeding consisting of a rapid rise followed by a return to baseline PP levels.  A lack of response to sham feeding can be caused by vagal nerve damage, autonomic disorders, or extensive pancreatic destruction (by pancreatitis or cancer).  [PMID:12146997]

[96420-5] Daratumumab [Presence] in Serum by Immunofixation -- **Component:** Daratumumab **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Immunofixation  **Short name:** Daratumumab Ser Ql IFE  **Definition:** Daratumumab is a monoclonal antibody used to treat multiple myeloma, which is, itself, a disease that results in antibody overproduction, shown as M protein band in immunoelectrophoresis. Daratumumab, the treatment, can show up as part of the M spike and interfere with the assay for the M-protein in multiple myeloma. An antibody against Daratumumab can be added to the assay, which shifts the Daratumumab band, allowing the Daratumumab band to be distinguished from the M-protein band and reported separately.

[96450-2] Hepatotocellular carcinoma risk [Score] GALAD -- **Component:** Hepatocellular carcinoma risk **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** GALAD  **Short name:** GALAD Score  **Definition:** The GALAD score provides the probability of a patient having hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC).  It is calculated based on gender, age, alpha-fetoprotein (AFP)-L3, AFP, and des-gamma-carboxy prothrombin [DCP], and higher GALAD model scores correlate with increased risk of HCC. The GALAD model is reported to be more accurate in diagnosing HCC compared to the use of the individual markers.

[96463-5] Copper free/Total copper in Serum or Plasma by calculation -- **Component:** Copper.free/Copper.total **Property:** MFr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated  **Short name:** Copper free/Total copper MFr SerPl Calc  **Definition:** A free copper level is calculated based on the difference between total copper and ceruloplasmin measurements, where the ceruloplasmin level represents the bound portion of circulating copper. In patients with Wilson's disease that is pre-symptomatic or adequately controlled, the free copper level is within normal range, but in symptomatic patients, it is typically elevated. Increasing free copper levels in a patient with previously normal levels may indicate a change in compliance with therapy or a growth spurt or change in diet requiring therapy adjustment. [PMID: 12662398] This term represents the calculated result for free copper level as a fraction of the total copper measurement.

[96568-1] Toxocara sp IgG Ab [Presence] in Serum by Immunoassay -- **Component:** Toxocara sp Ab.IgG **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** Ord **Method:** IA  **Short name:** Toxocara IgG Ser Ql IA
[96569-9] LACE index panel -- **Component:** LACE index panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** LACE index  **Short name:** LACE index panel  **Definition:** The LACE index can be used to predict the risk of hospital readmission or death within 30 days of hospital discharge in the general population. It is comprised of four elements: L - length of stay, A - acuity of admission, C - comorbidities, and E - Emergency department use. Scores from the four elements are summed for an overall LACE score which ranges from 0-19. Higher scores are associated with greater risk of hospital readmission and/or death within 30 days of hospital discharge. [PMID: 20194559]
[96571-5] Admitted via emergency department -- **Component:** Admitted via emergency department **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** ED Admit
[96574-9] LACE index score LACE index -- **Component:** LACE index score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** LACE index  **Short name:** LACE score LACE  **Definition:** Scores from the four LACE index elements (L - length of stay, A - acuity of admission, C - comorbidities, and E - Emergency department use) are summed for an overall Lace index score which ranges from 0-19. Higher scores are associated with greater risk of hospital readmission and/or death within 30 days of hospital discharge. [PMID: 20194559]

[96603-6] SARS-CoV-2 (COVID-19) S protein RBD neutralizing antibody [Presence] in Serum or Plasma by sVNT -- **Component:** SARS coronavirus 2 spike protein RBD Ab.neut **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Ord **Method:** sVNT  **Short name:** SARS-CoV-2 S RBD NAb SerPl Ql sVNT  **Definition:** Qualitative results for detection of neutralizing antibodies (NAbs) that block the binding of the S (spike) protein receptor binding domain (RDB) from SARS Coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) to ACE2 receptor protein in serum or plasma specimens by surrogate virus neutralization testing (sVNT), which is an immunoassay-based method. Neutralizing antibody testing is performed to determine whether the antibodies produced after SARS-CoV-2 infection or in response to a vaccination are able to neutralize the virus and prevent infection or re-infection. Different methods can be used to determine the presence of neutralizing antibodies, including conventional VNT (virus neutralization test, cVNT), pseudovirus VNT (pVNT), and surrogate VNT (sVNT). The sVNT method currently employs purified proteins in an immunoassay-based format, takes less time to complete, and does not require the use of live virus compared to cVNT and pVNT methods.

[96607-7] Blood pressure panel mean systolic and mean diastolic -- **Component:** Blood pressure panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** XXX^mean **System:** Arterial system **Scale:** -  **Short name:** BP Pnl Avg Sys+Avg Dias  **Definition:** Average (mean) Blood Pressure (systolic and diastolic) is the average of two or more blood pressure (BP) readings in a specified time period or according to a specified algorithm or protocol.  During clinical encounters 2 or more BPs should be averaged to more accurately assess a person's BP status.  When patients are self-monitoring blood pressure (SMBP), the American Heart Association recommends a minimum of 12 readings collected over at least 3 days should be averaged to more accurately assess a person's BP status (https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/10.1161/HYP.0000000000000087, PMID: 30827125).  According to the European Society of Hypertension , "The adverse cardiovascular consequences of hypertension, including events and mortality, largely depend on increased average BP values. Thus, decision-making in hypertension is based on average values of several BP readings obtained in and out of the office" (https://journals.lww.com/jhypertension/Fulltext/2021/07000/2021_European_Society_of_Hypertension_practice.5.aspx, PMID: 33710173).

[96616-8] HLA-A unresolved alleles [Type] in Donor by High resolution -- **Component:** HLA-A unresolved alleles **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss^Donor **Scale:** Nom **Method:** High resolution  **Short name:** HLA-A Unreslvd Alleles Donr High Res  **Definition:** HLA unresolved alleles are alleles that have ambiguous typing assignments; it's a way of listing which allele assignments are uncertain relevant to the level of resolution. It has been recommended that reports include all uncertainty in the HLA typing assignment relevant to the level of resolution as stated. As such, this term is intended to report the genotypes and/or alleles that have not been excluded and therefore, should be listed in the report [PMID:22001389].  Due to the variability in reporting and updates, example answer lists are not provided, however, the following resource can provide examples of expected answers- https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ipd/imgt/hla/ambig.html.

[96644-0] HLA-DPA1 unresolved alleles [Type] in Donor by High resolution -- **Component:** HLA-DPA1 unresolved alleles **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss^Donor **Scale:** Nom **Method:** High resolution  **Short name:** HLA-DPA1 Unreslvd Alleles Donr High Res  **Definition:** HLA unresolved alleles are alleles that have ambiguous typing assignments; it's a way of listing which allele assignments are uncertain relevant to the level of resolution. It has been recommended that reports include all uncertainty in the HLA typing assignment relevant to the level of resolution as stated. As such, this term is intended to report the genotypes and/or alleles that have not been excluded and therefore, should be listed in the report [PMID:22001389]. [96649-9] HLA-DPB1 T-cell epitope match [Type] in Donor+Patient by High resolution -- **Component:** HLA-DPB1 T-cell epitope match **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld/Tiss^Donor+Patient **Scale:** Nom **Method:** High resolution  **Short name:** HLA-DPB1 Ept Match Don+Pt High Res  **Definition:** Matching of HLA alleles between unrelated donors and transplant recipients is undertaken to reduce the risk of graft versus host disease to the greatest extent possible.  However, it has been shown that HLA-DPB1 matched unrelated donors are often difficult to find. As a result HLA-DPB1 alleles have been placed into groups, so that HLA-DPB1 mismatches between donor and recipient can be classified as "permissive mismatches" (which have a greater chance of success) versus "non-permissive" [PMID:22340965].

[96685-3] Clot formation and Platelet function.glass bead induced post heparin neutralization panel - Blood by Microviscometry -- **Component:** Clot formation & Platelet function.glass bead induced post heparin neutralization panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** - **Method:** Microviscometry  **Short name:** Clt Frm+PltFn Gls -Hep Pnl Bld MicrVisc  **Definition:** The glass bead induced clot formation by microviscometry panel is intended for general purpose global hemostasis monitoring. The monitoring information is typically used for screening for coagulopathies, and hyperfibrinolysis screening. This LOINC should be used for assay that include heparin capable of neutralizing therapeutic heparin at some level. This panel is not intended for high dose heparin management during cardiopulmonary bypass surgery.
[96686-1] Clot formation and Platelet function.glass bead induced panel - Blood by Microviscometry -- **Component:** Clot formation & Platelet function.glass bead induced panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** - **Method:** Microviscometry  **Short name:** Clt Frm+Plt Fnc Gls Ind Pnl Bld MicrVisc  **Definition:** The glass bead induced clot formation by microviscometry panel is intended for general purpose global hemostasis monitoring. This panel is not intended for high does heparin management during cardiopulmonary bypass surgery, however, this panel dose include a clot retraction result to assess platelet function.

[96722-4] Amyloid bodies [#/area] in Prostatic fluid by Light microscopy -- **Component:** Amyloid bodies **Property:** Naric **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Prostatic fld **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Microscopy.light  **Short name:** Amyloid #/area Prost fld Micro  **Definition:** Amyloid bodies, or corpora amylacea, are opaque, rounded bodies found in the acini of the prostate and occasionally in urine.  Abnormal deposits of amyloid bodies, or amyloidosis, can be harmful.  Older men often develop amyloid in the ducts of their prostates (corpora amylacea), that could underlie some cases of Benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH).  It has been suggested in the literature that there may be a link between bacterial infection, inflammation and amyloid deposition of pro-inflammatory proteins in the prostate, and (with some speculation) that these may contribute to the development of malignancy (Yanamandra K, et al. (2009) Amyloid Formation by the Pro-Inflammatory S100A8/A9 Proteins in the Ageing Prostate. PLoS ONE 4(5): e5562. doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0005562).

Higher scores are associated with greater independence and therefore greater potential for successful rehabilitation. [PMID: 14258950]

Whole-genome sequences of SARS-CoV-2 were made available on GISAID beginning in January 2020 to enable global genomic epidemiology and real-time surveillance of new COVID-19 viral strains. [https://www.gisaid.org/]
